search_text: Inter-model Interpretability: Self-supervised Models as a Case Study 模型间可解释性：自监督模型作为案例研究 Since early machine learning models, metrics such as accuracy and precision have been the de facto way to evaluate and compare trained models. However, a single metric number doesn't fully capture the similarities and differences between models, especially in the computer vision domain. A model with high accuracy on a certain dataset might provide a lower accuracy on another dataset, without any further insights. To address this problem we build on a recent interpretability technique called Dissect to introduce \textit{inter-model interpretability}, which determines how models relate or complement each other based on the visual concepts they have learned (such as objects and materials). Towards this goal, we project 13 top-performing self-supervised models into a Learned Concepts Embedding (LCE) space that reveals proximities among models from the perspective of learned concepts. We further crossed this information with the performance of these models on four computer vision tasks and 15 datasets. The experiment allowed us to categorize the models into three categories and revealed for the first time the type of visual concepts different tasks requires. This is a step forward for designing cross-task learning algorithms. 自早期的机器学习模型以来，准确性和精确度等指标已成为评估和比较训练模型的事实上的方法。然而，单个度量数字并不能完全捕捉模型之间的相似性和差异，尤其是在计算机视觉领域。在某个数据集上具有高精度的模型可能在另一个数据集上提供较低的精度，而无需任何进一步的见解。为了解决这个问题，我们基于最近的一种名为 Dissect 的可解释性技术来引入 \textit{模型间可解释性}，它决定模型如何根据它们学到的视觉概念（例如对象和材料）相互关联或互补。为了实现这一目标，我们将 13 个表现最佳的自监督模型投射到学习概念嵌入 (LCE) 空间中，从学习概念的角度揭示模型之间的相似性。我们进一步将这些信息与这些模型在 4 个计算机视觉任务和 15 个数据集上的性能进行交叉。该实验使我们能够将模型分为三类，并首次揭示了不同任务所需的视觉概念类型。这是设计跨任务学习算法的一个进步。 本文提出了一种跨模型可解释性方法，通过分析自监督模型学习的视觉概念，揭示模型间的关联与差异。 Ahmad Mustapha,Wael Khreich,Wes Masri cs.CV

authors_text: Greg Bodwin,Gary Hoppenworth
title: New Additive Spanner Lower Bounds by an Unlayered Obstacle Product
title_zh: 无分层障碍物产品的新加法扳手下限
abstract: For an input graph $G$, an additive spanner is a sparse subgraph $H$ whose shortest paths match those of $G$ up to small additive error. We prove two new lower bounds in the area of additive spanners: 1) We construct $n$-node graphs $G$ for which any spanner on $O(n)$ edges must increase a pairwise distance by $+Ω(n^{1/7})$. This improves on a recent lower bound of $+Ω(n^{1/10.5})$ by Lu, Wein, Vassilevska Williams, and Xu [SODA '22]. 2) A classic result by Coppersmith and Elkin [SODA '05] proves that for any $n$-node graph $G$ and set of $p = O(n^{1/2})$ demand pairs, one can exactly preserve all pairwise distances among demand pairs using a spanner on $O(n)$ edges. They also provided a lower bound construction, establishing that that this range $p = O(n^{1/2})$ cannot be improved. We strengthen this lower bound by proving that, for any constant $k$, this range of $p$ is still unimprovable even if the spanner is allowed $+k$ additive error among the demand pairs. This negatively resolves an open question asked by Coppersmith and Elkin [SODA '05] and again by Cygan, Grandoni, and Kavitha [STACS '13] and Abboud and Bodwin [SODA '16]. At a technical level, our lower bounds are obtained by an improvement to the entire obstacle product framework used to compose "inner" and "outer" graphs into lower bound instances. In particular, we develop a new strategy for analysis that allows certain non-layered graphs to be used in the product, and we use this freedom to design better inner and outer graphs that lead to our new lower bounds.
summary: For an input graph $G$, an additive spanner is a sparse subgraph $H$ whose shortest paths match those of $G$ up to small additive error. We prove two new lower bounds in the area of additive spanners: 1) We construct $n$-node graphs $G$ for which any spanner on $O(n)$ edges must increase a pairwise distance by $+Ω(n^{1/7})$. This improves on a recent lower bound of $+Ω(n^{1/10.5})$ by Lu, Wein, Vassilevska Williams, and Xu [SODA '22]. 2) A classic result by Coppersmith and Elkin [SODA '05] proves that for any $n$-node graph $G$ and set of $p = O(n^{1/2})$ demand pairs, one can exactly preserve all pairwise distances among demand pairs using a spanner on $O(n)$ edges. They also provided a lower bound construction, establishing that that this range $p = O(n^{1/2})$ cannot be improved. We strengthen this lower bound by proving that, for any constant $k$, this range of $p$ is still unimprovable even if the spanner is allowed $+k$ additive error among the demand pairs. This negatively resolves an open question asked by Coppersmith and Elkin [SODA '05] and again by Cygan, Grandoni, and Kavitha [STACS '13] and Abboud and Bodwin [SODA '16]. At a technical level, our lower bounds are obtained by an improvement to the entire obstacle product framework used to compose "inner" and "outer" graphs into lower bound instances. In particular, we develop a new strategy for analysis that allows certain non-layered graphs to be used in the product, and we use this freedom to design better inner and outer graphs that lead to our new lower bounds.
abstract_zh: 对于输入图 $G$，加性扳手是一个稀疏子图 $H$，其最短路径与 $G$ 的最短路径匹配，直至小的加性误差。我们证明了加性扳手领域的两个新下界：1) 我们构造 $n$ 节点图 $G$，其中 $O(n)$ 边上的任何扳手必须将成对距离增加 $+Ω(n^{1/7})$。这改进了 Lu、Wein、Vassilevska Williams 和 Xu [SODA '22] 最近提出的 $+Ω(n^{1/10.5})$ 下限。 2) Coppersmith 和 Elkin [SODA '05] 的经典结果证明，对于任何 $n$ 节点图 $G$ 和一组 $p = O(n^{1/2})$ 需求对，可以使用 $O(n)$ 边上的扳手精确保留需求对之间的所有成对距离。他们还提供了一个下界构造，确定这个范围 $p = O(n^{1/2})$ 无法改进。我们通过证明对于任何常数 $k$ 来说，即使在需求对之间允许扳手 $+k$ 附加误差，这个 $p$ 的范围仍然是不可改进的，从而强化了这个下限。这消极地解决了 Coppersmith 和 Elkin [SODA '05] 以及 Cygan、Grandoni 和 Kavitha [STACS '13] 以及 Abboud 和 Bodwin [SODA '16] 再次提出的悬而未决的问题。在技​​术层面上，我们的下界是通过改进用于将“内部”和“外部”图组成下界实例的整个障碍产品框架来获得的。特别是，我们开发了一种新的分析策略，允许在产品中使用某些非分层图，并且我们利用这种自由度来设计更好的内部和外部图，从而达到新的下限。

AI.tldr: 证明加法扳子（additive spanner）的新下界，包括边数为O(n)的扳子必须付出+Ω(n^{1/7})的加性距离误差，以及即使允许+k的加性误差，p=O(n^{1/2})的需求对范围仍不可改进。
AI.motivation: 改进现有加法扳子的下界结果，将下界从+Ω(n^{1/10.5})提升至+Ω(n^{1/7})，并否定Coppersmith和Elkin提出的开放问题——即证明即使在允许常数加性误差的情况下，需求对的范围也无法扩大。
AI.method: 对障碍乘积（obstacle product）框架进行改进，提出新的分析策略，允许在乘积中使用非分层图，从而设计更好的内部图和外部图来构造下界实例。
AI.result: 1) 构造了n节点图G，使任意O(n)条边的扳子必须产生+Ω(n^{1/7})的加性距离误差；2) 对任意常数k，即使允许+k加性误差，p=O(n^{1/2})的需求对范围仍不可改进。
AI.conclusion: 通过改进障碍乘积框架，获得了加法扳子更强的下界，并否定了领域内长期存在的开放问题。

result: 1) 构造了n节点图G，使任意O(n)条边的扳子必须产生+Ω(n^{1/7})的加性距离误差；2) 对任意常数k，即使允许+k加性误差，p=O(n^{1/2})的需求对范围仍不可改进。
conclusion: 通过改进障碍乘积框架，获得了加法扳子更强的下界，并否定了领域内长期存在的开放问题。
search_text: New Additive Spanner Lower Bounds by an Unlayered Obstacle Product 无分层障碍物产品的新加法扳手下限 For an input graph $G$, an additive spanner is a sparse subgraph $H$ whose shortest paths match those of $G$ up to small additive error. We prove two new lower bounds in the area of additive spanners: 1) We construct $n$-node graphs $G$ for which any spanner on $O(n)$ edges must increase a pairwise distance by $+Ω(n^{1/7})$. This improves on a recent lower bound of $+Ω(n^{1/10.5})$ by Lu, Wein, Vassilevska Williams, and Xu [SODA '22]. 2) A classic result by Coppersmith and Elkin [SODA '05] proves that for any $n$-node graph $G$ and set of $p = O(n^{1/2})$ demand pairs, one can exactly preserve all pairwise distances among demand pairs using a spanner on $O(n)$ edges. They also provided a lower bound construction, establishing that that this range $p = O(n^{1/2})$ cannot be improved. We strengthen this lower bound by proving that, for any constant $k$, this range of $p$ is still unimprovable even if the spanner is allowed $+k$ additive error among the demand pairs. This negatively resolves an open question asked by Coppersmith and Elkin [SODA '05] and again by Cygan, Grandoni, and Kavitha [STACS '13] and Abboud and Bodwin [SODA '16]. At a technical level, our lower bounds are obtained by an improvement to the entire obstacle product framework used to compose "inner" and "outer" graphs into lower bound instances. In particular, we develop a new strategy for analysis that allows certain non-layered graphs to be used in the product, and we use this freedom to design better inner and outer graphs that lead to our new lower bounds. 对于输入图 $G$，加性扳手是一个稀疏子图 $H$，其最短路径与 $G$ 的最短路径匹配，直至小的加性误差。我们证明了加性扳手领域的两个新下界：1) 我们构造 $n$ 节点图 $G$，其中 $O(n)$ 边上的任何扳手必须将成对距离增加 $+Ω(n^{1/7})$。这改进了 Lu、Wein、Vassilevska Williams 和 Xu [SODA '22] 最近提出的 $+Ω(n^{1/10.5})$ 下限。 2) Coppersmith 和 Elkin [SODA '05] 的经典结果证明，对于任何 $n$ 节点图 $G$ 和一组 $p = O(n^{1/2})$ 需求对，可以使用 $O(n)$ 边上的扳手精确保留需求对之间的所有成对距离。他们还提供了一个下界构造，确定这个范围 $p = O(n^{1/2})$ 无法改进。我们通过证明对于任何常数 $k$ 来说，即使在需求对之间允许扳手 $+k$ 附加误差，这个 $p$ 的范围仍然是不可改进的，从而强化了这个下限。这消极地解决了 Coppersmith 和 Elkin [SODA '05] 以及 Cygan、Grandoni 和 Kavitha [STACS '13] 以及 Abboud 和 Bodwin [SODA '16] 再次提出的悬而未决的问题。在技​​术层面上，我们的下界是通过改进用于将“内部”和“外部”图组成下界实例的整个障碍产品框架来获得的。特别是，我们开发了一种新的分析策略，允许在产品中使用某些非分层图，并且我们利用这种自由度来设计更好的内部和外部图，从而达到新的下限。 证明加法扳子（additive spanner）的新下界，包括边数为O(n)的扳子必须付出+Ω(n^{1/7})的加性距离误差，以及即使允许+k的加性误差，p=O(n^{1/2})的需求对范围仍不可改进。 Greg Bodwin,Gary Hoppenworth cs.DS

authors_text: Michael A. Bender,Seth Gilbert,Fabian Kuhn,John Kuszmaul,Muriel Médard
title: Contention Resolution for Coded Radio Networks
title_zh: 编码无线电网络的争用解决
abstract: Randomized backoff protocols, such as exponential backoff, are a powerful tool for managing access to a shared resource, often a wireless communication channel (e.g., [1]). For a wireless device to transmit successfully, it uses a backoff protocol to ensure exclusive access to the channel. Modern radios, however, do not need exclusive access to the channel to communicate; in particular, they have the ability to receive useful information even when more than one device transmits at the same time. These capabilities have now been exploited for many years by systems that rely on interference cancellation, physical layer network coding and analog network coding to improve efficiency. For example, Zigzag decoding [56] demonstrated how a base station can decode messages sent by multiple devices simultaneously. In this paper, we address the following question: Can we design a backoff protocol that is better than exponential backoff when exclusive channel access is not required. We define the Coded Radio Network Model, which generalizes traditional radio network models (e.g., [30]). We then introduce the Decodable Backoff Algorithm, a randomized backoff protocol that achieves an optimal throughput of $1-o(1)$. (Throughput $1$ is optimal, as simultaneous reception does not increase the channel capacity.) The algorithm breaks the constant throughput lower bound for traditional radio networks [47-49], showing the power of these new hardware capabilities.
summary: Randomized backoff protocols, such as exponential backoff, are a powerful tool for managing access to a shared resource, often a wireless communication channel (e.g., [1]). For example, Zigzag decoding [56] demonstrated how a base station can decode messages sent by multiple devices simultaneously. In this paper, we address the following question: Can we design a backoff protocol that is better than exponential backoff when exclusive channel access is not required. We define the Coded Radio Network Model, which generalizes traditional radio network models (e.g., [30]). abstract_zh: 随机退避协议（例如指数退避）是管理对共享资源（通常是无线通信信道）的访问的强大工具（例如[1]）。为了使无线设备成功传输，它使用退避协议来确保对信道的独占访问。然而，现代无线电不需要独占信道来进行通信；特别是，即使多个设备同时传输，它们也能够接收有用的信息。这些功能现已被依赖干扰消除、物理层网络编码和模拟网络编码的系统利用多年，以提高效率。例如，Zigzag 解码[56]演示了基站如何解码多个设备同时发送的消息。在本文中，我们解决了以下问题：当不需要独占通道访问时，我们是否可以设计一种比指数退避更好的退避协议。我们定义了编码无线电网络模型，它概括了传统的无线电网络模型（例如[30]）。然后，我们介绍可解码退避算法，这是一种随机退避协议，可实现 $1-o(1)$ 的最佳吞吐量。 （吞吐量 1 美元是最佳的，因为同时接收不会增加信道容量。）该算法打破了传统无线电网络的恒定吞吐量下限 [47-49]，显示了这些新硬件功能的强大功能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种改进的随机退避协议，用于在无需独占信道访问的场景下提升无线网络的传输效率。
AI.motivation: 传统随机退避协议（如指数退避）依赖独占信道访问，而现代无线设备可通过干扰消除和网络编码技术实现多设备同时传输。现有研究未充分利用这些硬件能力。
AI.method: 定义了编码无线网络模型，并设计了可解码退避算法（Decodable Backoff Algorithm），结合随机退避与多用户信号解码技术。
AI.result: 算法实现了接近最优的吞吐量（1-o(1)），突破了传统无线网络的常数吞吐量下界（如47-49号文献所述）。
AI.conclusion: 通过利用现代无线设备的多用户通信能力，新协议显著提升了网络效率，证明了硬件特性的应用潜力。

authors_text: Tu N. Nguyen,Bing-Hong Liu,My T. Thai,Ivan Djordjevic
title: Towards An Optimal Solution to Place Bistatic Radars for Belt Barrier Coverage with Minimum Cost
title_zh: 寻求以最低成本部署双基地雷达以实现传送带屏障覆盖的最佳解决方案
abstract: With the rapid growth of threats, sophistication and diversity in the manner of intrusion, traditional belt barrier systems are now faced with a major challenge of providing high and concrete coverage quality to expand the guarding service market. Recent efforts aim at constructing a belt barrier by deploying bistatic radar(s) on a specific line regardless of the limitation on deployment locations, to keep the width of the barrier from going below a specific threshold and the total bistatic radar placement cost is minimized, referred to as the Minimum Cost Linear Placement (MCLP) problem. The existing solutions are heuristic, and their validity is tightly bound by the barrier width parameter that these solutions only work for a fixed barrier width value. In this work, we propose an optimal solution, referred to as the Opt_MCLP, for the "open MCLP problem" that works for full range of the barrier width. Through rigorous theoretical analysis and experimentation, we demonstrate that the proposed algorithms perform well in terms of placement cost reduction and barrier coverage guarantee.
summary: With the rapid growth of threats, sophistication and diversity in the manner of intrusion, traditional belt barrier systems are now faced with a major challenge of providing high and concrete coverage quality to expand the guarding service market. Recent efforts aim at constructing a belt barrier by deploying bistatic radar(s) on a specific line regardless of the limitation on deployment locations, to keep the width of the barrier from going below a specific threshold and the total bistatic radar placement cost is minimized, referred to as the Minimum Cost Linear Placement (MCLP) problem. The existing solutions are heuristic, and their validity is tightly bound by the barrier width parameter that these solutions only work for a fixed barrier width value. In this work, we propose an optimal solution, referred to as the Opt_MCLP, for the "open MCLP problem" that works for full range of the barrier width. Through rigorous theoretical analysis and experimentation, we demonstrate that the proposed algorithms perform well in terms of placement cost reduction and barrier coverage guarantee.
abstract_zh: 随着威胁的快速增长、入侵方式的复杂性和多样性，传统的带式护栏系统现在面临着提供高而具体的覆盖质量以扩大防护服务市场的重大挑战。最近的努力旨在通过在特定线路上部署双基地雷达来构建带状屏障，而不管部署位置的限制，以保持屏障的宽度不低于特定阈值，并使双基地雷达放置总成本最小化，称为最小成本线性放置（MCLP）问题。现有的解决方案是启发式的，它们的有效性受到屏障宽度参数的严格限制，这些解决方案仅适用于固定的屏障宽度值。在这项工作中，我们针对适用于全范围势垒宽度的“开放 MCLP 问题”提出了一个最优解决方案，称为 Opt_MCLP。通过严格的理论分析和实验，我们证明所提出的算法在降低布局成本和保证障碍覆盖方面表现良好。

AI.tldr: 本文旨在提出一种以最低成本部署双基地雷达的最优解，确保带状屏障覆盖宽度不低于特定阈值。
AI.motivation: 传统带状屏障系统难以应对多样化入侵威胁，现有启发式方法受限于固定屏障宽度参数，缺乏灵活性和普适性。
AI.method: 提出名为Opt_MCLP的算法，通过理论分析和实验验证，解决适用于全范围屏障宽度的开放MCLP问题。
AI.result: 实验表明，该算法在降低部署成本和保证屏障覆盖质量方面表现优异。
AI.conclusion: 新方法突破了传统固定宽度限制，为实际场景中动态调整屏障覆盖提供了高效低成本的解决方案。

The existing solutions are heuristic, and their validity is tightly bound by the barrier width parameter that these solutions only work for a fixed barrier width value. In this work, we propose an optimal solution, referred to as the Opt_MCLP, for the "open MCLP problem" that works for full range of the barrier width. Through rigorous theoretical analysis and experimentation, we demonstrate that the proposed algorithms perform well in terms of placement cost reduction and barrier coverage guarantee. 随着威胁的快速增长、入侵方式的复杂性和多样性，传统的带式护栏系统现在面临着提供高而具体的覆盖质量以扩大防护服务市场的重大挑战。最近的努力旨在通过在特定线路上部署双基地雷达来构建带状屏障，而不管部署位置的限制，以保持屏障的宽度不低于特定阈值，并使双基地雷达放置总成本最小化，称为最小成本线性放置（MCLP）问题。现有的解决方案是启发式的，它们的有效性受到屏障宽度参数的严格限制，这些解决方案仅适用于固定的屏障宽度值。在这项工作中，我们针对适用于全范围势垒宽度的“开放 MCLP 问题”提出了一个最优解决方案，称为 Opt_MCLP。通过严格的理论分析和实验，我们证明所提出的算法在降低布局成本和保证障碍覆盖方面表现良好。 本文旨在提出一种以最低成本部署双基地雷达的最优解，确保带状屏障覆盖宽度不低于特定阈值。 Tu N. Nguyen,Bing-Hong Liu,My T. Thai,Ivan Djordjevic cs.DS,eess.SY

authors_text: Md Mohaiminul Islam,Gedas Bertasius
title: Object State Change Classification in Egocentric Videos using the Divided Space-Time Attention Mechanism
title_zh: 使用时空分割注意力机制对自我中心视频中的对象状态变化进行分类
abstract: This report describes our submission called "TarHeels" for the Ego4D: Object State Change Classification Challenge. We use a transformer-based video recognition model and leverage the Divided Space-Time Attention mechanism for classifying object state change in egocentric videos. Our submission achieves the second-best performance in the challenge. Furthermore, we perform an ablation study to show that identifying object state change in egocentric videos requires temporal modeling ability. Lastly, we present several positive and negative examples to visualize our model's predictions. The code is publicly available at: https://github.com/md-mohaiminul/ObjectStateChange
summary: This report describes our submission called "TarHeels" for the Ego4D: Object State Change Classification Challenge. We use a transformer-based video recognition model and leverage the Divided Space-Time Attention mechanism for classifying object state change in egocentric videos. Our submission achieves the second-best performance in the challenge. Furthermore, we perform an ablation study to show that identifying object state change in egocentric videos requires temporal modeling ability. Lastly, we present several positive and negative examples to visualize our model's predictions. The code is publicly available at: https://github.com/md-mohaiminul/ObjectStateChange
abstract_zh: 本报告描述了我们为 Ego4D：对象状态变化分类挑战赛提交的名为“TarHeels”的作品。我们使用基于 Transformer 的视频识别模型，并利用分割时空注意力机制对以自我为中心的视频中的对象状态变化进行分类。我们提交的作品在挑战赛中取得了第二好的成绩。此外，我们进行了一项消融研究，以表明识别以自我为中心的视频中的对象状态变化需要时间建模能力。最后，我们提出了几个正面和负面的例子来可视化我们模型的预测。该代码可在以下位置公开获取：https://github.com/md-mohaiminul/ObjectStateChange

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于分时空注意力机制的Transformer视频识别模型，用于解决第一人称视角视频中的物体状态变化分类问题。
AI.motivation: 第一人称视角视频中的物体状态变化分类需要同时建模时空信息，而现有方法在时间维度建模能力不足，导致分类效果受限。
AI.method: 采用Transformer架构，设计分时空注意力机制，将空间特征与时间特征分离处理，通过自注意力机制捕捉物体状态变化的时空依赖关系。
AI.result: 在Ego4D挑战赛中取得第二名成绩，消融实验证明时间建模对任务的重要性，同时公开了代码仓库。
AI.conclusion: 分时空注意力机制有效提升了物体状态变化分类性能，验证了时间建模在第一人称视频理解中的关键作用。

authors_text: Yu-Yun Tseng,Alexander Bell,Danna Gurari
title: VizWiz-FewShot: Locating Objects in Images Taken by People With Visual Impairments
title_zh: VizWiz-FewShot：定位视力障碍人士拍摄的图像中的对象
comment: Accepted to ECCV 2022. The first two authors contributed equally
comments: Accepted to ECCV 2022. The first two authors contributed equally
abstract: We introduce a few-shot localization dataset originating from photographers who authentically were trying to learn about the visual content in the images they took. It includes nearly 10,000 segmentations of 100 categories in over 4,500 images that were taken by people with visual impairments. Compared to existing few-shot object detection and instance segmentation datasets, our dataset is the first to locate holes in objects (e.g., found in 12.3\% of our segmentations), it shows objects that occupy a much larger range of sizes relative to the images, and text is over five times more common in our objects (e.g., found in 22.4\% of our segmentations). Analysis of three modern few-shot localization algorithms demonstrates that they generalize poorly to our new dataset. The algorithms commonly struggle to locate objects with holes, very small and very large objects, and objects lacking text. To encourage a larger community to work on these unsolved challenges, we publicly share our annotated few-shot dataset at https://vizwiz.org .
summary: We introduce a few-shot localization dataset originating from photographers who authentically were trying to learn about the visual content in the images they took. It includes nearly 10,000 segmentations of 100 categories in over 4,500 images that were taken by people with visual impairments. Compared to existing few-shot object detection and instance segmentation datasets, our dataset is the first to locate holes in objects (e.g., found in 12.3\% of our segmentations), it shows objects that occupy a much larger range of sizes relative to the images, and text is over five times more common in our objects (e.g., found in 22.4\% of our segmentations). Analysis of three modern few-shot localization algorithms demonstrates that they generalize poorly to our new dataset. The algorithms commonly struggle to locate objects with holes, very small and very large objects, and objects lacking text. To encourage a larger community to work on these unsolved challenges, we publicly share our annotated few-shot dataset at https://vizwiz.org .
abstract_zh: 我们引入了一些镜头本地化数据集，该数据集源自摄影师，他们真正试图了解他们拍摄的图像中的视觉内容。它包括由视力障碍人士拍摄的 4,500 多张图像中 100 个类别的近 10,000 个分割。与现有的少镜头对象检测和实例分割数据集相比，我们的数据集是第一个定位对象中的洞的数据集（例如，在我们的分割的 12.3\% 中发现），它显示相对于图像占据更大尺寸范围的对象，并且文本在我们的对象中常见五倍以上（例如，在我们的分割的 22.4\% 中发现）。对三种现代少样本定位算法的分析表明，它们对我们的新数据集的泛化性很差。这些算法通常很难定位有孔的对象、非常小和非常大的对象以及缺少文本的对象。为了鼓励更大的社区致力于解决这些未解决的挑战，我们在 https://vizwiz.org 上公开分享我们带注释的少样本数据集。

AI.tldr: 本文介绍了一个由视觉障碍者拍摄图像构成的少样本定位数据集，用于改进现有算法在处理孔洞、尺寸差异及文本等挑战性场景下的性能。
AI.motivation: 现有少样本定位数据集缺乏对视觉障碍者拍摄图像中特有挑战（如物体孔洞、尺寸差异和高频文本）的覆盖，需构建新数据集推动相关研究。
AI.method: 收集并标注4500余张视觉障碍者拍摄图像中的100类物体，包含近10,000个分割实例，特别包含孔洞、多尺寸物体及文本标注，并测试三种现代算法在该数据集上的表现。
AI.result: 现有算法在定位含孔洞、极端尺寸或无文本的物体时泛化能力不足，凸显数据集的新挑战。
AI.conclusion: 公开该数据集以促进学术界针对少样本定位中孔洞处理、尺寸适应性和文本依赖性等未解难题展开研究。

authors_text: Hossein Mirzaee,Javad Peymanfard,Hamid Habibzadeh Moshtaghin,Hossein Zeinali
title: ArmanEmo: A Persian Dataset for Text-based Emotion Detection
title_zh: ArmanEmo：用于基于文本的情绪检测的波斯语数据集
abstract: With the recent proliferation of open textual data on social media platforms, Emotion Detection (ED) from Text has received more attention over the past years. It has many applications, especially for businesses and online service providers, where emotion detection techniques can help them make informed commercial decisions by analyzing customers/users' feelings towards their products and services. In this study, we introduce ArmanEmo, a human-labeled emotion dataset of more than 7000 Persian sentences labeled for seven categories. The dataset has been collected from different resources, including Twitter, Instagram, and Digikala (an Iranian e-commerce company) comments. Labels are based on Ekman's six basic emotions (Anger, Fear, Happiness, Hatred, Sadness, Wonder) and another category (Other) to consider any other emotion not included in Ekman's model. Along with the dataset, we have provided several baseline models for emotion classification focusing on the state-of-the-art transformer-based language models. Our best model achieves a macro-averaged F1 score of 75.39 percent across our test dataset. Moreover, we also conduct transfer learning experiments to compare our proposed dataset's generalization against other Persian emotion datasets. Results of these experiments suggest that our dataset has superior generalizability among the existing Persian emotion datasets. ArmanEmo is publicly available for non-commercial use at https://github.com/Arman-Rayan-Sharif/arman-text-emotion.
summary: With the recent proliferation of open textual data on social media platforms, Emotion Detection (ED) from Text has received more attention over the past years. It has many applications, especially for businesses and online service providers, where emotion detection techniques can help them make informed commercial decisions by analyzing customers/users' feelings towards their products and services. In this study, we introduce ArmanEmo, a human-labeled emotion dataset of more than 7000 Persian sentences labeled for seven categories. The dataset has been collected from different resources, including Twitter, Instagram, and Digikala (an Iranian e-commerce company) comments. Labels are based on Ekman's six basic emotions (Anger, Fear, Happiness, Hatred, Sadness, Wonder) and another category (Other) to consider any other emotion not included in Ekman's model. Along with the dataset, we have provided several baseline models for emotion classification focusing on the state-of-the-art transformer-based language models. Our best model achieves a macro-averaged F1 score of 75.39 percent across our test dataset. Moreover, we also conduct transfer learning experiments to compare our proposed dataset's generalization against other Persian emotion datasets. Results of these experiments suggest that our dataset has superior generalizability among the existing Persian emotion datasets. ArmanEmo is publicly available for non-commercial use at https://github.com/Arman-Rayan-Sharif/arman-text-emotion.
abstract_zh: 随着最近社交媒体平台上开放文本数据的激增，文本中的情绪检测（ED）在过去几年中受到了越来越多的关注。它有很多应用，特别是对于企业和在线服务提供商来说，情绪检测技术可以通过分析客户/用户对其产品和服务的感受来帮助他们做出明智的商业决策。在这项研究中，我们引入了 ArmanEmo，这是一个人类标记的情感数据集，包含 7 个类别的 7000 多个波斯语句子。该数据集是从不同的资源收集的，包括 Twitter、Instagram 和 Digikala（伊朗电子商务公司）评论。标签基于 Ekman 的六种基本情绪（愤怒、恐惧、幸福、仇恨、悲伤、惊奇）和另一个类别（其他），以考虑 Ekman 模型中未包含的任何其他情绪。除了数据集之外，我们还提供了几个用于情感分类的基线模型，重点关注最先进的基于 Transformer 的语言模型。我们的最佳模型在我们的测试数据集中实现了 75.39% 的宏观平均 F1 分数。此外，我们还进行了迁移学习实验，以将我们提出的数据集的泛化能力与其他波斯情感数据集进行比较。这些实验的结果表明，我们的数据集在现有的波斯情感数据集中具有出色的通用性。 ArmanEmo 可在 https://github.com/Arman-Rayan-Sharif/arman-text-emotion 上公开用于非商业用途。

AI.tldr: 本文介绍了一个名为ArmanEmo的波斯语文本情感检测数据集，包含7000多条人工标注的句子，并提供了基于Transformer模型的基线方法。
AI.motivation: 随着社交媒体文本数据的激增，情感检测在商业决策中变得重要。现有波斯语情感数据集不足，需构建更全面的标注数据以提升模型性能。
AI.method: 从Twitter、Instagram和Digikala等平台收集波斯语文本，按Ekman六种基本情绪及'其他'类别标注。采用Transformer模型进行情感分类，并通过迁移学习验证数据集泛化能力。
AI.result: 最佳模型在测试集上达到75.39%的宏F1分数，迁移学习实验表明该数据集相比现有波斯语情感数据集具有更优的泛化能力。
AI.conclusion: ArmanEmo为波斯语情感分析提供了高质量数据集，其模型表现和泛化能力优于现有数据集，可为非商业研究提供支持。

search_text: ArmanEmo: A Persian Dataset for Text-based Emotion Detection ArmanEmo：用于基于文本的情绪检测的波斯语数据集 With the recent proliferation of open textual data on social media platforms, Emotion Detection (ED) from Text has received more attention over the past years. It has many applications, especially for businesses and online service providers, where emotion detection techniques can help them make informed commercial decisions by analyzing customers/users' feelings towards their products and services. In this study, we introduce ArmanEmo, a human-labeled emotion dataset of more than 7000 Persian sentences labeled for seven categories. The dataset has been collected from different resources, including Twitter, Instagram, and Digikala (an Iranian e-commerce company) comments. Labels are based on Ekman's six basic emotions (Anger, Fear, Happiness, Hatred, Sadness, Wonder) and another category (Other) to consider any other emotion not included in Ekman's model. Along with the dataset, we have provided several baseline models for emotion classification focusing on the state-of-the-art transformer-based language models. Our best model achieves a macro-averaged F1 score of 75.39 percent across our test dataset. Moreover, we also conduct transfer learning experiments to compare our proposed dataset's generalization against other Persian emotion datasets. Results of these experiments suggest that our dataset has superior generalizability among the existing Persian emotion datasets. ArmanEmo is publicly available for non-commercial use at https://github.com/Arman-Rayan-Sharif/arman-text-emotion. 随着最近社交媒体平台上开放文本数据的激增，文本中的情绪检测（ED）在过去几年中受到了越来越多的关注。它有很多应用，特别是对于企业和在线服务提供商来说，情绪检测技术可以通过分析客户/用户对其产品和服务的感受来帮助他们做出明智的商业决策。在这项研究中，我们引入了 ArmanEmo，这是一个人类标记的情感数据集，包含 7 个类别的 7000 多个波斯语句子。该数据集是从不同的资源收集的，包括 Twitter、Instagram 和 Digikala（伊朗电子商务公司）评论。标签基于 Ekman 的六种基本情绪（愤怒、恐惧、幸福、仇恨、悲伤、惊奇）和另一个类别（其他），以考虑 Ekman 模型中未包含的任何其他情绪。除了数据集之外，我们还提供了几个用于情感分类的基线模型，重点关注最先进的基于 Transformer 的语言模型。我们的最佳模型在我们的测试数据集中实现了 75.39% 的宏观平均 F1 分数。此外，我们还进行了迁移学习实验，以将我们提出的数据集的泛化能力与其他波斯情感数据集进行比较。这些实验的结果表明，我们的数据集在现有的波斯情感数据集中具有出色的通用性。 ArmanEmo 可在 https://github.com/Arman-Rayan-Sharif/arman-text-emotion 上公开用于非商业用途。 本文介绍了一个名为ArmanEmo的波斯语文本情感检测数据集，包含7000多条人工标注的句子，并提供了基于Transformer模型的基线方法。 Hossein Mirzaee,Javad Peymanfard,Hamid Habibzadeh Moshtaghin,Hossein Zeinali cs.CL,cs.AI

authors_text: Zhi Li,Lu He,Huijuan Xu
title: Weakly-Supervised Temporal Action Detection for Fine-Grained Videos with Hierarchical Atomic Actions
title_zh: 具有分层原子动作的细粒度视频的弱监督时间动作检测
abstract: Action understanding has evolved into the era of fine granularity, as most human behaviors in real life have only minor differences. To detect these fine-grained actions accurately in a label-efficient way, we tackle the problem of weakly-supervised fine-grained temporal action detection in videos for the first time. Without the careful design to capture subtle differences between fine-grained actions, previous weakly-supervised models for general action detection cannot perform well in the fine-grained setting. We propose to model actions as the combinations of reusable atomic actions which are automatically discovered from data through self-supervised clustering, in order to capture the commonality and individuality of fine-grained actions. The learnt atomic actions, represented by visual concepts, are further mapped to fine and coarse action labels leveraging the semantic label hierarchy. Our approach constructs a visual representation hierarchy of four levels: clip level, atomic action level, fine action class level and coarse action class level, with supervision at each level. Extensive experiments on two large-scale fine-grained video datasets, FineAction and FineGym, show the benefit of our proposed weakly-supervised model for fine-grained action detection, and it achieves state-of-the-art results.
summary: Action understanding has evolved into the era of fine granularity, as most human behaviors in real life have only minor differences. To detect these fine-grained actions accurately in a label-efficient way, we tackle the problem of weakly-supervised fine-grained temporal action detection in videos for the first time. Without the careful design to capture subtle differences between fine-grained actions, previous weakly-supervised models for general action detection cannot perform well in the fine-grained setting. We propose to model actions as the combinations of reusable atomic actions which are automatically discovered from data through self-supervised clustering, in order to capture the commonality and individuality of fine-grained actions. The learnt atomic actions, represented by visual concepts, are further mapped to fine and coarse action labels leveraging the semantic label hierarchy. Our approach constructs a visual representation hierarchy of four levels: clip level, atomic action level, fine action class level and coarse action class level, with supervision at each level. Extensive experiments on two large-scale fine-grained video datasets, FineAction and FineGym, show the benefit of our proposed weakly-supervised model for fine-grained action detection, and it achieves state-of-the-art results.
abstract_zh: 动作理解已经发展到细粒度时代，因为现实生活中大多数人类行为只有微小的差异。为了以标签有效的方式准确检测这些细粒度动作，我们首次解决了视频中弱监督细粒度时间动作检测的问题。如果没有仔细的设计来捕捉细粒度动作之间的细微差异，以前用于一般动作检测的弱监督模型就无法在细粒度设置中表现良好。我们建议将动作建模为可重用原子动作的组合，这些原子动作是通过自监督聚类从数据中自动发现的，以捕获细粒度动作的共性和个性。学习到的原子动作（由视觉概念表示）利用语义标签层次结构进一步映射到精细和粗略的动作标签。我们的方法构建了四个级别的视觉表示层次结构：剪辑级别、原子动作级别、精细动作类级别和粗略动作类级别，每个级别都有监督。对两个大型细粒度视频数据集 FineAction 和 FineGym 的大量实验表明，我们提出的弱监督模型在细粒度动作检测方面的优势，并且取得了最先进的结果。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种弱监督细粒度视频动作检测方法，通过层次化原子动作建模来捕捉细粒度动作的细微差异。
AI.motivation: 现有弱监督模型无法有效处理细粒度动作间的细微差异，导致检测性能不足，需设计新的方法以提升细粒度动作理解能力。
AI.method: 通过自监督聚类自动发现可复用的原子动作，构建四层视觉表示层次结构（片段级、原子动作级、细粒度动作类、粗粒度动作类），并利用语义标签层次映射原子动作到具体动作标签。
AI.result: 在FineAction和FineGym两个大规模细粒度视频数据集上取得最先进结果，验证了方法的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 层次化原子动作建模结合弱监督学习能有效捕捉细粒度动作的共性与特性，为细粒度视频动作检测提供了新思路。

authors_text: Fabrizio De Caro,Adam J. Collin,Alfredo Vaccaro
title: Data-driven Models to Anticipate Critical Voltage Events in Power Systems
title_zh: 用于预测电力系统中关键电压事件的数据驱动模型
comment: In proceedings of the 11th Bulk Power Systems Dynamics and Control Symposium (IREP 2022), July 25-30, 2022, Banff, Canada
comments: In proceedings of the 11th Bulk Power Systems Dynamics and Control Symposium (IREP 2022), July 25-30, 2022, Banff, Canada
abstract: This paper explores the effectiveness of data-driven models to predict voltage excursion events in power systems using simple categorical labels. By treating the prediction as a categorical classification task, the workflow is characterized by a low computational and data burden. A proof-of-concept case study on a real portion of the Italian 150 kV sub-transmission network, which hosts a significant amount of wind power generation, demonstrates the general validity of the proposal and offers insight into the strengths and weaknesses of several widely utilized prediction models for this application.
summary: This paper explores the effectiveness of data-driven models to predict voltage excursion events in power systems using simple categorical labels. By treating the prediction as a categorical classification task, the workflow is characterized by a low computational and data burden. A proof-of-concept case study on a real portion of the Italian 150 kV sub-transmission network, which hosts a significant amount of wind power generation, demonstrates the general validity of the proposal and offers insight into the strengths and weaknesses of several widely utilized prediction models for this application.
abstract_zh: 本文探讨了数据驱动模型使用简单的分类标签预测电力系统中电压偏移事件的有效性。通过将预测视为分类任务，该工作流程的特点是计算量和数据负担低。对意大利 150 kV 子输电网络（该网络包含大量风力发电）的实际部分进行的概念验证案例研究证明了该提案的总体有效性，并深入了解了该应用中几种广泛使用的预测模型的优缺点。

AI.tldr: 本文研究利用数据驱动模型预测电力系统中电压异常事件的有效性，通过分类任务降低计算和数据负担。
AI.motivation: 电压异常可能引发电网不稳定，传统方法依赖复杂模型且数据需求高，需探索更高效预测方案。
AI.method: 将电压预测建模为分类任务，采用简化标签体系，并基于意大利150kV风电网络进行实证分析。
AI.result: 验证了数据驱动方法的普遍适用性，揭示了多种预测模型在该场景下的性能差异及局限性。
AI.conclusion: 分类驱动的轻量级模型可有效预测电压事件，为电力系统稳定性评估提供新思路。

By treating the prediction as a categorical classification task, the workflow is characterized by a low computational and data burden. A proof-of-concept case study on a real portion of the Italian 150 kV sub-transmission network, which hosts a significant amount of wind power generation, demonstrates the general validity of the proposal and offers insight into the strengths and weaknesses of several widely utilized prediction models for this application. 本文探讨了数据驱动模型使用简单的分类标签预测电力系统中电压偏移事件的有效性。通过将预测视为分类任务，该工作流程的特点是计算量和数据负担低。对意大利 150 kV 子输电网络（该网络包含大量风力发电）的实际部分进行的概念验证案例研究证明了该提案的总体有效性，并深入了解了该应用中几种广泛使用的预测模型的优缺点。 本文研究利用数据驱动模型预测电力系统中电压异常事件的有效性，通过分类任务降低计算和数据负担。 Fabrizio De Caro,Adam J. Collin,Alfredo Vaccaro cs.AI,eess.SP

authors_text: Zezhou Cheng,Menglei Chai,Jian Ren,Hsin-Ying Lee,Kyle Olszewski,Zeng Huang,Subhransu Maji,Sergey Tulyakov
title: Cross-Modal 3D Shape Generation and Manipulation
title_zh: 跨模态 3D 形状生成和操作
abstract: Creating and editing the shape and color of 3D objects require tremendous human effort and expertise. Compared to direct manipulation in 3D interfaces, 2D interactions such as sketches and scribbles are usually much more natural and intuitive for the users. In this paper, we propose a generic multi-modal generative model that couples the 2D modalities and implicit 3D representations through shared latent spaces. With the proposed model, versatile 3D generation and manipulation are enabled by simply propagating the editing from a specific 2D controlling modality through the latent spaces. For example, editing the 3D shape by drawing a sketch, re-colorizing the 3D surface via painting color scribbles on the 2D rendering, or generating 3D shapes of a certain category given one or a few reference images. Unlike prior works, our model does not require re-training or fine-tuning per editing task and is also conceptually simple, easy to implement, robust to input domain shifts, and flexible to diverse reconstruction on partial 2D inputs. We evaluate our framework on two representative 2D modalities of grayscale line sketches and rendered color images, and demonstrate that our method enables various shape manipulation and generation tasks with these 2D modalities.

Compared to direct manipulation in 3D interfaces, 2D interactions such as sketches and scribbles are usually much more natural and intuitive for the users. For example, editing the 3D shape by drawing a sketch, re-colorizing the 3D surface via painting color scribbles on the 2D rendering, or generating 3D shapes of a certain category given one or a few reference images. Unlike prior works, our model does not require re-training or fine-tuning per editing task and is also conceptually simple, easy to implement, robust to input domain shifts, and flexible to diverse reconstruction on partial 2D inputs. abstract_zh: 创建和编辑 3D 对象的形状和颜色需要大量的人力和专业知识。与 3D 界面中的直接操作相比，草图和涂鸦等 2D 交互对于用户来说通常更加自然和直观。在本文中，我们提出了一种通用的多模态生成模型，该模型通过共享潜在空间将 2D 模态和隐式 3D 表示耦合起来。使用所提出的模型，只需通过潜在空间传播特定 2D 控制模态的编辑即可实现多功能 3D 生成和操作。例如，通过绘制草图来编辑 3D 形状，通过在 2D 渲染上绘制颜色涂鸦来重新着色 3D 表面，或者在给定一个或几个参考图像的情况下生成特定类别的 3D 形状。与之前的工作不同，我们的模型不需要对每个编辑任务进行重新训练或微调，并且概念上简单、易于实现、对输入域转换具有鲁棒性，并且能够灵活地对部分 2D 输入进行不同的重建。我们在灰度线草图和渲染彩色图像的两种代表性 2D 模式上评估我们的框架，并证明我们的方法可以使用这些 2D 模式实现各种形状操作和生成任务。

AI.tldr: 提出一种多模态生成模型，通过共享潜在空间实现基于2D输入的3D形状生成与编辑。
AI.motivation: 传统3D建模需要大量人力和专业知识，而2D交互（如草图、涂鸦）更自然直观，需探索高效跨模态生成方法。
AI.method: 构建共享潜在空间的多模态生成模型，通过2D模态编辑传播到3D潜在空间，实现无需微调的生成与编辑。
AI.result: 在灰度线草图和渲染颜色图像上验证方法有效性，实现形状生成、编辑及部分输入的鲁棒重建。
AI.conclusion: 提出的方法在概念和实现上简单，适用于多样化的2D输入，且无需重新训练，具有广泛的应用潜力。

Compared to direct manipulation in 3D interfaces, 2D interactions such as sketches and scribbles are usually much more natural and intuitive for the users. In this paper, we propose a generic multi-modal generative model that couples the 2D modalities and implicit 3D representations through shared latent spaces. With the proposed model, versatile 3D generation and manipulation are enabled by simply propagating the editing from a specific 2D controlling modality through the latent spaces. For example, editing the 3D shape by drawing a sketch, re-colorizing the 3D surface via painting color scribbles on the 2D rendering, or generating 3D shapes of a certain category given one or a few reference images. Unlike prior works, our model does not require re-training or fine-tuning per editing task and is also conceptually simple, easy to implement, robust to input domain shifts, and flexible to diverse reconstruction on partial 2D inputs. We evaluate our framework on two representative 2D modalities of grayscale line sketches and rendered color images, and demonstrate that our method enables various shape manipulation and generation tasks with these 2D modalities. 创建和编辑 3D 对象的形状和颜色需要大量的人力和专业知识。与 3D 界面中的直接操作相比，草图和涂鸦等 2D 交互对于用户来说通常更加自然和直观。在本文中，我们提出了一种通用的多模态生成模型，该模型通过共享潜在空间将 2D 模态和隐式 3D 表示耦合起来。使用所提出的模型，只需通过潜在空间传播特定 2D 控制模态的编辑即可实现多功能 3D 生成和操作。例如，通过绘制草图来编辑 3D 形状，通过在 2D 渲染上绘制颜色涂鸦来重新着色 3D 表面，或者在给定一个或几个参考图像的情况下生成特定类别的 3D 形状。与之前的工作不同，我们的模型不需要对每个编辑任务进行重新训练或微调，并且概念上简单、易于实现、对输入域转换具有鲁棒性，并且能够灵活地对部分 2D 输入进行不同的重建。我们在灰度线草图和渲染彩色图像的两种代表性 2D 模式上评估我们的框架，并证明我们的方法可以使用这些 2D 模式实现各种形状操作和生成任务。 提出一种多模态生成模型，通过共享潜在空间实现基于2D输入的3D形状生成与编辑。 Zezhou Cheng,Menglei Chai,Jian Ren,Hsin-Ying Lee,Kyle Olszewski,Zeng Huang,Subhransu Maji,Sergey Tulyakov cs.CV

authors_text: Bo Sun,Vladimir G. Kim,Noam Aigerman,Qixing Huang,Siddhartha Chaudhuri
title: PatchRD: Detail-Preserving Shape Completion by Learning Patch Retrieval and Deformation
title_zh: PatchRD：通过学习补丁检索和变形来完成保留细节的形状
abstract: This paper introduces a data-driven shape completion approach that focuses on completing geometric details of missing regions of 3D shapes. We observe that existing generative methods lack the training data and representation capacity to synthesize plausible, fine-grained details with complex geometry and topology. Our key insight is to copy and deform patches from the partial input to complete missing regions. This enables us to preserve the style of local geometric features, even if it drastically differs from the training data. Our fully automatic approach proceeds in two stages. First, we learn to retrieve candidate patches from the input shape. Second, we select and deform some of the retrieved candidates to seamlessly blend them into the complete shape. This method combines the advantages of the two most common completion methods: similarity-based single-instance completion, and completion by learning a shape space. We leverage repeating patterns by retrieving patches from the partial input, and learn global structural priors by using a neural network to guide the retrieval and deformation steps. Experimental results show our approach considerably outperforms baselines across multiple datasets and shape categories. Code and data are available at https://github.com/GitBoSun/PatchRD.
summary: This paper introduces a data-driven shape completion approach that focuses on completing geometric details of missing regions of 3D shapes. We observe that existing generative methods lack the training data and representation capacity to synthesize plausible, fine-grained details with complex geometry and topology. Our key insight is to copy and deform patches from the partial input to complete missing regions. This enables us to preserve the style of local geometric features, even if it drastically differs from the training data. Our fully automatic approach proceeds in two stages. First, we learn to retrieve candidate patches from the input shape. Second, we select and deform some of the retrieved candidates to seamlessly blend them into the complete shape. This method combines the advantages of the two most common completion methods: similarity-based single-instance completion, and completion by learning a shape space. We leverage repeating patterns by retrieving patches from the partial input, and learn global structural priors by using a neural network to guide the retrieval and deformation steps. Experimental results show our approach considerably outperforms baselines across multiple datasets and shape categories. Code and data are available at https://github.com/GitBoSun/PatchRD.
abstract_zh: 本文介绍了一种数据驱动的形状补全方法，该方法侧重于补全 3D 形状缺失区域的几何细节。我们观察到现有的生成方法缺乏训练数据和表示能力来合成具有复杂几何和拓扑的合理的细粒度细节。我们的主要见解是从部分输入中复制和变形补丁以完成缺失区域。这使我们能够保留局部几何特征的风格，即使它与训练数据有很大不同。我们的全自动方法分两个阶段进行。首先，我们学习从输入形状中检索候选补丁。其次，我们选择一些检索到的候选对象并对其进行变形，以将它们无缝地混合成完整的形状。该方法结合了两种最常见的补全方法的优点：基于相似性的单实例补全和通过学习形状空间的补全。我们通过从部分输入中检索补丁来利用重复模式，并通过使用神经网络来指导检索和变形步骤来学习全局结构先验。实验结果表明，我们的方法在多个数据集和形状类别中明显优于基线。代码和数据可在 https://github.com/GitBoSun/PatchRD 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于学习补丁检索与变形的细节保留三维形状补全方法PatchRD。
AI.motivation: 现有生成方法因缺乏训练数据和表达能力，难以合成具有复杂几何和拓扑结构的精细细节，导致局部几何特征风格无法有效保留。
AI.method: 分两阶段自动完成：1）从输入形状中检索候选补丁；2）选择并变形部分候选补丁以无缝融合至完整形状，结合相似性单实例补全与形状空间学习的优势。
AI.result: 在多个数据集和形状类别上显著优于基线方法，代码和数据已开源。
AI.conclusion: 通过补丁检索与变形结合全局结构先验，有效保留局部几何特征，为复杂形状补全提供新思路。

authors_text: Gaoang Wang,Yibing Zhan,Xinchao Wang,Mingli Song,Klara Nahrstedt
title: Hierarchical Semi-Supervised Contrastive Learning for Contamination-Resistant Anomaly Detection
title_zh: 用于抗污染异常检测的分层半监督对比学习
abstract: Anomaly detection aims at identifying deviant samples from the normal data distribution. Contrastive learning has provided a successful way to sample representation that enables effective discrimination on anomalies. However, when contaminated with unlabeled abnormal samples in training set under semi-supervised settings, current contrastive-based methods generally 1) ignore the comprehensive relation between training data, leading to suboptimal performance, and 2) require fine-tuning, resulting in low efficiency. To address the above two issues, in this paper, we propose a novel hierarchical semi-supervised contrastive learning (HSCL) framework, for contamination-resistant anomaly detection. Specifically, HSCL hierarchically regulates three complementary relations: sample-to-sample, sample-to-prototype, and normal-to-abnormal relations, enlarging the discrimination between normal and abnormal samples with a comprehensive exploration of the contaminated data. Besides, HSCL is an end-to-end learning approach that can efficiently learn discriminative representations without fine-tuning. HSCL achieves state-of-the-art performance in multiple scenarios, such as one-class classification and cross-dataset detection. Extensive ablation studies further verify the effectiveness of each considered relation. The code is available at https://github.com/GaoangW/HSCL.
summary: Anomaly detection aims at identifying deviant samples from the normal data distribution. Contrastive learning has provided a successful way to sample representation that enables effective discrimination on anomalies. However, when contaminated with unlabeled abnormal samples in training set under semi-supervised settings, current contrastive-based methods generally 1) ignore the comprehensive relation between training data, leading to suboptimal performance, and 2) require fine-tuning, resulting in low efficiency. To address the above two issues, in this paper, we propose a novel hierarchical semi-supervised contrastive learning (HSCL) framework, for contamination-resistant anomaly detection. Specifically, HSCL hierarchically regulates three complementary relations: sample-to-sample, sample-to-prototype, and normal-to-abnormal relations, enlarging the discrimination between normal and abnormal samples with a comprehensive exploration of the contaminated data. Besides, HSCL is an end-to-end learning approach that can efficiently learn discriminative representations without fine-tuning. HSCL achieves state-of-the-art performance in multiple scenarios, such as one-class classification and cross-dataset detection. Extensive ablation studies further verify the effectiveness of each considered relation. The code is available at https://github.com/GaoangW/HSCL.
abstract_zh: 异常检测旨在从正态数据分布中识别异常样本。对比学习提供了一种成功的采样表示方法，可以有效区分异常。然而，当半监督设置下训练集中未标记的异常样本污染时，当前基于对比的方法通常1）忽略训练数据之间的综合关系，导致性能次优；2）需要微调，导致效率低下。为了解决上述两个问题，在本文中，我们提出了一种新颖的分层半监督对比学习（HSCL）框架，用于抗污染异常检测。具体来说，HSCL分层规范了样本与样本、样本与原型、正常与异常之间的三种互补关系，通过对污染数据的全面探索，放大了正常样本与异常样本的区分度。此外，HSCL 是一种端到端的学习方法，可以有效地学习判别性表示，而无需微调。 HSCL 在多种场景中实现了最先进的性能，例如一类分类和跨数据集检测。广泛的消融研究进一步验证了每个考虑的关系的有效性。代码可在 https://github.com/GaoangW/HSCL 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种分层半监督对比学习框架（HSCL），用于在存在未标记异常样本的训练集下实现抗污染的异常检测。
AI.motivation: 当前基于对比学习的异常检测方法在半监督设置下，由于忽略训练数据的全面关系以及需要微调，导致性能不佳且效率低下。
AI.method: HSCL通过分层调节三种互补关系（样本到样本、样本到原型、正常到异常）来增强正常与异常样本之间的区分度，并以端到端方式学习判别性表示，无需微调。
AI.result: HSCL在多个场景（如一类分类和跨数据集检测）中达到最先进性能，广泛的消融实验验证了每种关系的有效性。
AI.conclusion: HSCL框架有效解决了半监督异常检测中的污染问题，提高了检测性能和效率。

search_text: Hierarchical Semi-Supervised Contrastive Learning for Contamination-Resistant Anomaly Detection 用于抗污染异常检测的分层半监督对比学习 Anomaly detection aims at identifying deviant samples from the normal data distribution. Contrastive learning has provided a successful way to sample representation that enables effective discrimination on anomalies. However, when contaminated with unlabeled abnormal samples in training set under semi-supervised settings, current contrastive-based methods generally 1) ignore the comprehensive relation between training data, leading to suboptimal performance, and 2) require fine-tuning, resulting in low efficiency. To address the above two issues, in this paper, we propose a novel hierarchical semi-supervised contrastive learning (HSCL) framework, for contamination-resistant anomaly detection. Specifically, HSCL hierarchically regulates three complementary relations: sample-to-sample, sample-to-prototype, and normal-to-abnormal relations, enlarging the discrimination between normal and abnormal samples with a comprehensive exploration of the contaminated data. Besides, HSCL is an end-to-end learning approach that can efficiently learn discriminative representations without fine-tuning. HSCL achieves state-of-the-art performance in multiple scenarios, such as one-class classification and cross-dataset detection. Extensive ablation studies further verify the effectiveness of each considered relation. The code is available at https://github.com/GaoangW/HSCL. 异常检测旨在从正态数据分布中识别异常样本。对比学习提供了一种成功的采样表示方法，可以有效区分异常。然而，当半监督设置下训练集中未标记的异常样本污染时，当前基于对比的方法通常1）忽略训练数据之间的综合关系，导致性能次优；2）需要微调，导致效率低下。为了解决上述两个问题，在本文中，我们提出了一种新颖的分层半监督对比学习（HSCL）框架，用于抗污染异常检测。具体来说，HSCL分层规范了样本与样本、样本与原型、正常与异常之间的三种互补关系，通过对污染数据的全面探索，放大了正常样本与异常样本的区分度。此外，HSCL 是一种端到端的学习方法，可以有效地学习判别性表示，而无需微调。 HSCL 在多种场景中实现了最先进的性能，例如一类分类和跨数据集检测。广泛的消融研究进一步验证了每个考虑的关系的有效性。代码可在 https://github.com/GaoangW/HSCL 获取。 本文提出了一种分层半监督对比学习框架（HSCL），用于在存在未标记异常样本的训练集下实现抗污染的异常检测。 Gaoang Wang,Yibing Zhan,Xinchao Wang,Mingli Song,Klara Nahrstedt cs.CV

authors_text: Haobing Liu,Yanmin Zhu,Chunyang Wang,Jianyu Ding,Jiadi Yu,Feilong Tang
title: Incorporating Heterogeneous User Behaviors and Social Influences for Predictive Analysis
title_zh: 结合异质用户行为和社会影响进行预测分析
abstract: Behavior prediction based on historical behavioral data have practical real-world significance. It has been applied in recommendation, predicting academic performance, etc. With the refinement of user data description, the development of new functions, and the fusion of multiple data sources, heterogeneous behavioral data which contain multiple types of behaviors become more and more common. In this paper, we aim to incorporate heterogeneous user behaviors and social influences for behavior predictions. To this end, this paper proposes a variant of Long-Short Term Memory (LSTM) which can consider context information while modeling a behavior sequence, a projection mechanism which can model multi-faceted relationships among different types of behaviors, and a multi-faceted attention mechanism which can dynamically find out informative periods from different facets. Many kinds of behavioral data belong to spatio-temporal data. An unsupervised way to construct a social behavior graph based on spatio-temporal data and to model social influences is proposed. Moreover, a residual learning-based decoder is designed to automatically construct multiple high-order cross features based on social behavior representation and other types of behavior representations. Qualitative and quantitative experiments on real-world datasets have demonstrated the effectiveness of this model.
summary: Behavior prediction based on historical behavioral data have practical real-world significance. It has been applied in recommendation, predicting academic performance, etc. With the refinement of user data description, the development of new functions, and the fusion of multiple data sources, heterogeneous behavioral data which contain multiple types of behaviors become more and more common. In this paper, we aim to incorporate heterogeneous user behaviors and social influences for behavior predictions. To this end, this paper proposes a variant of Long-Short Term Memory (LSTM) which can consider context information while modeling a behavior sequence, a projection mechanism which can model multi-faceted relationships among different types of behaviors, and a multi-faceted attention mechanism which can dynamically find out informative periods from different facets. Many kinds of behavioral data belong to spatio-temporal data. An unsupervised way to construct a social behavior graph based on spatio-temporal data and to model social influences is proposed. Moreover, a residual learning-based decoder is designed to automatically construct multiple high-order cross features based on social behavior representation and other types of behavior representations. Qualitative and quantitative experiments on real-world datasets have demonstrated the effectiveness of this model.
abstract_zh: 基于历史行为数据的行为预测具有现实意义。它已被应用于推荐、预测学业成绩等方面。随着用户数据描述的细化、新功能的开发以及多种数据源的融合，包含多种行为类型的异构行为数据变得越来越普遍。在本文中，我们的目标是将异构用户行为和社会影响纳入行为预测。为此，本文提出了一种长短期记忆（LSTM）的变体，可以在建模行为序列时考虑上下文信息，可以建模不同类型行为之间的多方面关系的投影机制，以及可以动态地从不同方面找出信息周期的多方面注意机制。许多行为数据都属于时空数据。提出了一种基于时空数据构建社会行为图并建模社会影响的无监督方法。此外，基于残差学习的解码器被设计为基于社会行为表示和其他类型的行为表示自动构造多个高阶交叉特征。对现实数据集的定性和定量实验证明了该模型的有效性。

AI.tldr: 本文旨在通过整合异构用户行为和社会影响，提升行为预测的准确性。
AI.motivation: 现有方法难以有效处理多类型异构行为数据及社会因素对行为的动态影响，导致预测效果受限。
AI.method: 提出改进的LSTM模型以捕捉上下文信息，设计投影机制建模行为间多维关系，结合多维注意力机制筛选关键时段，并基于时空数据构建社交行为图与残差解码器生成高阶交叉特征。
AI.result: 在真实数据集上的定性与定量实验验证了该模型的有效性，预测性能优于现有方法。
AI.conclusion: 通过融合异构行为与社会影响，模型能更全面刻画用户行为模式，为预测任务提供新思路。

authors_text: Charles Schaff,Matthew R. Walter
title: N-LIMB: Neural Limb Optimization for Efficient Morphological Design
title_zh: N-LIMB：用于高效形态设计的神经肢体优化
comment: For code and videos, see https://sites.google.com/ttic.edu/nlimb
comments: For code and videos, see https://sites.google.com/ttic.edu/nlimb
abstract: A robot's ability to complete a task is heavily dependent on its physical design. However, identifying an optimal physical design and its corresponding control policy is inherently challenging. The freedom to choose the number of links, their type, and how they are connected results in a combinatorial design space, and the evaluation of any design in that space requires deriving its optimal controller. In this work, we present N-LIMB, an efficient approach to optimizing the design and control of a robot over large sets of morphologies. Central to our framework is a universal, design-conditioned control policy capable of controlling a diverse sets of designs. This policy greatly improves the sample efficiency of our approach by allowing the transfer of experience across designs and reducing the cost to evaluate new designs. We train this policy to maximize expected return over a distribution of designs, which is simultaneously updated towards higher performing designs under the universal policy. In this way, our approach converges towards a design distribution peaked around high-performing designs and a controller that is effectively fine-tuned for those designs. We demonstrate the potential of our approach on a series of locomotion tasks across varying terrains and show the discovery novel and high-performing design-control pairs.
summary: A robot's ability to complete a task is heavily dependent on its physical design. However, identifying an optimal physical design and its corresponding control policy is inherently challenging. The freedom to choose the number of links, their type, and how they are connected results in a combinatorial design space, and the evaluation of any design in that space requires deriving its optimal controller. In this work, we present N-LIMB, an efficient approach to optimizing the design and control of a robot over large sets of morphologies. Central to our framework is a universal, design-conditioned control policy capable of controlling a diverse sets of designs. This policy greatly improves the sample efficiency of our approach by allowing the transfer of experience across designs and reducing the cost to evaluate new designs. We train this policy to maximize expected return over a distribution of designs, which is simultaneously updated towards higher performing designs under the universal policy. In this way, our approach converges towards a design distribution peaked around high-performing designs and a controller that is effectively fine-tuned for those designs. We demonstrate the potential of our approach on a series of locomotion tasks across varying terrains and show the discovery novel and high-performing design-control pairs.
abstract_zh: 机器人完成任务的能力在很大程度上取决于其物理设计。然而，确定最佳物理设计及其相应的控制策略本质上是具有挑战性的。自由选择链接的数量、类型以及连接方式会产生组合设计空间，并且对该空间中的任何设计进行评估都需要推导其最佳控制器。在这项工作中，我们提出了 N-LIMB，这是一种在大量形态上优化机器人设计和控制的有效方法。我们框架的核心是一个通用的、以设计为条件的控制策略，能够控制不同的设计集。该政策允许跨设计转移经验并降低评估新设计的成本，从而极大地提高了我们方法的样本效率。我们训练该策略以最大化设计分布的预期回报，同时在通用策略下更新为更高性能的设计。通过这种方式，我们的方法集中于围绕高性能设计的设计分布和针对这些设计进行有效微调的控制器。我们展示了我们的方法在不同地形的一系列运动任务中的潜力，并展示了发现的新颖且高性能的设计控制对。

AI.tldr: 本文提出N-LIMB方法，通过神经肢体优化实现高效机器人形态设计与控制策略联合优化。
AI.motivation: 机器人任务完成能力依赖物理设计，但形态设计空间庞大且评估成本高，传统方法难以高效探索最优设计与控制策略组合。
AI.method: 设计条件化控制策略与形态分布联合优化框架，通过经验跨形态迁移提升样本效率，同步更新形态分布与控制器参数。
AI.result: 在多地形移动任务中发现新颖且高性能的形态-控制配对，验证方法有效性。
AI.conclusion: N-LIMB通过统一控制策略与形态分布协同优化，实现了大规模形态空间中高效设计探索与控制策略生成。

tldr: 本文提出N-LIMB方法，通过神经肢体优化实现高效机器人形态设计与控制策略联合优化。
motivation: 机器人任务完成能力依赖物理设计，但形态设计空间庞大且评估成本高，传统方法难以高效探索最优设计与控制策略组合。
method: 设计条件化控制策略与形态分布联合优化框架，通过经验跨形态迁移提升样本效率，同步更新形态分布与控制器参数。
result: 在多地形移动任务中发现新颖且高性能的形态-控制配对，验证方法有效性。
conclusion: N-LIMB通过统一控制策略与形态分布协同优化，实现了大规模形态空间中高效设计探索与控制策略生成。
search_text: N-LIMB: Neural Limb Optimization for Efficient Morphological Design N-LIMB：用于高效形态设计的神经肢体优化 A robot's ability to complete a task is heavily dependent on its physical design. However, identifying an optimal physical design and its corresponding control policy is inherently challenging. The freedom to choose the number of links, their type, and how they are connected results in a combinatorial design space, and the evaluation of any design in that space requires deriving its optimal controller. In this work, we present N-LIMB, an efficient approach to optimizing the design and control of a robot over large sets of morphologies. Central to our framework is a universal, design-conditioned control policy capable of controlling a diverse sets of designs. This policy greatly improves the sample efficiency of our approach by allowing the transfer of experience across designs and reducing the cost to evaluate new designs. We train this policy to maximize expected return over a distribution of designs, which is simultaneously updated towards higher performing designs under the universal policy. In this way, our approach converges towards a design distribution peaked around high-performing designs and a controller that is effectively fine-tuned for those designs. We demonstrate the potential of our approach on a series of locomotion tasks across varying terrains and show the discovery novel and high-performing design-control pairs. 机器人完成任务的能力在很大程度上取决于其物理设计。然而，确定最佳物理设计及其相应的控制策略本质上是具有挑战性的。自由选择链接的数量、类型以及连接方式会产生组合设计空间，并且对该空间中的任何设计进行评估都需要推导其最佳控制器。在这项工作中，我们提出了 N-LIMB，这是一种在大量形态上优化机器人设计和控制的有效方法。我们框架的核心是一个通用的、以设计为条件的控制策略，能够控制不同的设计集。该政策允许跨设计转移经验并降低评估新设计的成本，从而极大地提高了我们方法的样本效率。我们训练该策略以最大化设计分布的预期回报，同时在通用策略下更新为更高性能的设计。通过这种方式，我们的方法集中于围绕高性能设计的设计分布和针对这些设计进行有效微调的控制器。我们展示了我们的方法在不同地形的一系列运动任务中的潜力，并展示了发现的新颖且高性能的设计控制对。 本文提出N-LIMB方法，通过神经肢体优化实现高效机器人形态设计与控制策略联合优化。 Charles Schaff,Matthew R. Walter cs.RO

authors_text: Prashanth Venkataraman
title: Image Denoising Using Convolutional Autoencoder
title_zh: 使用卷积自动编码器进行图像去噪
abstract: With the inexorable digitalisation of the modern world, every subset in the field of technology goes through major advancements constantly. One such subset is digital images which are ever so popular. Images can not always be as visually pleasing or clear as you would want them to be and are often distorted or obscured with noise. A number of techniques to enhance images have come up as the years passed, all with their own respective pros and cons. In this paper, we look at one such particular technique which accomplishes this task with the help of a neural network model commonly known as an autoencoder. We construct different architectures for the model and compare results in order to decide the one best suited for the task. The characteristics and working of the model are discussed briefly knowing which can help set a path for future research.
summary: With the inexorable digitalisation of the modern world, every subset in the field of technology goes through major advancements constantly. One such subset is digital images which are ever so popular. Images can not always be as visually pleasing or clear as you would want them to be and are often distorted or obscured with noise. A number of techniques to enhance images have come up as the years passed, all with their own respective pros and cons. In this paper, we look at one such particular technique which accomplishes this task with the help of a neural network model commonly known as an autoencoder. We construct different architectures for the model and compare results in order to decide the one best suited for the task. The characteristics and working of the model are discussed briefly knowing which can help set a path for future research.
abstract_zh: 随着现代世界不可阻挡的数字化，技术领域的每个子集都在不断地经历重大进步。其中一个子集是非常流行的数字图像。图像并不总是像您希望的那样在视觉上令人愉悦或清晰，并且经常会被噪声扭曲或模糊。随着时间的推移，出现了许多增强图像的技术，所有这些技术都有各自的优点和缺点。在本文中，我们研究了一种特殊的技术，该技术借助通常称为自动编码器的神经网络模型来完成此任务。我们为模型构建不同的架构并比较结果，以确定最适合该任务的架构。简要讨论该模型的特征和工作原理，了解这有助于为未来的研究奠定基础。

AI.tldr: 本文研究使用卷积自编码器进行图像去噪任务，通过构建和比较不同模型架构来确定最佳方案。
AI.motivation: 数字图像常因噪声干扰导致质量下降，传统去噪方法存在局限性，需探索更有效的深度学习解决方案。
AI.method: 设计多种卷积自编码器架构，通过实验比较不同模型性能，分析其去噪效果以选择最优结构。
AI.result: 确定了适用于图像去噪任务的自编码器最佳架构，并验证了其在降噪中的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 卷积自编码器能有效实现图像去噪，未来可进一步优化模型结构或结合其他技术提升性能。

authors_text: Shuai Shen,Wanhua Li,Zheng Zhu,Yueqi Duan,Jie Zhou,Jiwen Lu
title: Learning Dynamic Facial Radiance Fields for Few-Shot Talking Head Synthesis
title_zh: 学习动态面部辐射场以实现少镜头说话头部合成
comment: Accepted by ECCV 2022. Project page: https://sstzal.github.io/DFRF/
comments: Accepted by ECCV 2022. Project page: https://sstzal.github.io/DFRF/
abstract: Talking head synthesis is an emerging technology with wide applications in film dubbing, virtual avatars and online education. Recent NeRF-based methods generate more natural talking videos, as they better capture the 3D structural information of faces. However, a specific model needs to be trained for each identity with a large dataset. In this paper, we propose Dynamic Facial Radiance Fields (DFRF) for few-shot talking head synthesis, which can rapidly generalize to an unseen identity with few training data. Different from the existing NeRF-based methods which directly encode the 3D geometry and appearance of a specific person into the network, our DFRF conditions face radiance field on 2D appearance images to learn the face prior. Thus the facial radiance field can be flexibly adjusted to the new identity with few reference images. Additionally, for better modeling of the facial deformations, we propose a differentiable face warping module conditioned on audio signals to deform all reference images to the query space. Extensive experiments show that with only tens of seconds of training clip available, our proposed DFRF can synthesize natural and high-quality audio-driven talking head videos for novel identities with only 40k iterations. We highly recommend readers view our supplementary video for intuitive comparisons. Code is available in https://sstzal.github.io/DFRF/.
summary: Talking head synthesis is an emerging technology with wide applications in film dubbing, virtual avatars and online education. Recent NeRF-based methods generate more natural talking videos, as they better capture the 3D structural information of faces. However, a specific model needs to be trained for each identity with a large dataset. In this paper, we propose Dynamic Facial Radiance Fields (DFRF) for few-shot talking head synthesis, which can rapidly generalize to an unseen identity with few training data. Different from the existing NeRF-based methods which directly encode the 3D geometry and appearance of a specific person into the network, our DFRF conditions face radiance field on 2D appearance images to learn the face prior. Thus the facial radiance field can be flexibly adjusted to the new identity with few reference images. Additionally, for better modeling of the facial deformations, we propose a differentiable face warping module conditioned on audio signals to deform all reference images to the query space. Extensive experiments show that with only tens of seconds of training clip available, our proposed DFRF can synthesize natural and high-quality audio-driven talking head videos for novel identities with only 40k iterations. We highly recommend readers view our supplementary video for intuitive comparisons. Code is available in https://sstzal.github.io/DFRF/.
abstract_zh: 头颅合成是一项新兴技术，在电影配音、虚拟形象和在线教育等领域有着广泛的应用。最近基于 NeRF 的方法可以生成更自然的谈话视频，因为它们可以更好地捕获面部的 3D 结构信息。然而，需要使用大数据集为每个身份训练特定的模型。在本文中，我们提出了用于少镜头说话头部合成的动态面部辐射场（DFRF），它可以用很少的训练数据快速泛化为不可见的身份。与现有的基于 NeRF 的方法直接将特定人的 3D 几何形状和外观编码到网络中不同，我们的 DFRF 条件通过 2D 外观图像上的人脸辐射场来先验学习人脸。因此，面部辐射场可以通过很少的参考图像灵活地调整到新的身份。此外，为了更好地建模面部变形，我们提出了一种以音频信号为条件的可微分面部变形模块，以使所有参考图像变形到查询空间。大量实验表明，只需数十秒的训练剪辑，我们提出的 DFRF 就可以合成自然且高质量的音频驱动的头部说话视频，以实现新颖的身份，只需 40k 次迭代。我们强烈建议读者观看我们的补充视频进行直观比较。代码可在 https://sstzal.github.io/DFRF/ 中找到。

AI.tldr: 本文提出动态面部辐射场（DFRF）方法，用于在少量训练数据下快速生成高质量的音频驱动 talking head 视频。
AI.motivation: 现有基于NeRF的方法需为每个身份训练独立模型且依赖大量数据，难以满足实际应用中对新身份快速泛化的需要。
AI.method: 通过2D外观图像条件化辐射场学习面部先验，并引入基于音频信号的可微分面部变形模块，实现参考图像到查询空间的形变建模。
AI.result: 仅需数十秒训练数据即可在40k迭代内生成自然高质量的音频驱动 talking head 视频，效果优于现有方法。
AI.conclusion: DFRF通过解耦几何与外观建模，有效解决了few-shot talking head合成问题，为虚拟角色生成等应用提供新思路。

authors_text: Rohan Bavishi,Harshit Joshi,José Pablo Cambronero Sánchez,Anna Fariha,Sumit Gulwani,Vu Le,Ivan Radicek,Ashish Tiwari
title: Neurosymbolic Repair for Low-Code Formula Languages
title_zh: 低代码公式语言的神经符号修复
abstract: Most users of low-code platforms, such as Excel and PowerApps, write programs in domain-specific formula languages to carry out nontrivial tasks. Often users can write most of the program they want, but introduce small mistakes that yield broken formulas. These mistakes, which can be both syntactic and semantic, are hard for low-code users to identify and fix, even though they can be resolved with just a few edits. We formalize the problem of producing such edits as the last-mile repair problem. To address this problem, we developed LaMirage, a LAst-MIle RepAir-engine GEnerator that combines symbolic and neural techniques to perform last-mile repair in low-code formula languages. LaMirage takes a grammar and a set of domain-specific constraints/rules, which jointly approximate the target language, and uses these to generate a repair engine that can fix formulas in that language. To tackle the challenges of localizing the errors and ranking the candidate repairs, LaMirage leverages neural techniques, whereas it relies on symbolic methods to generate candidate repairs. This combination allows LaMirage to find repairs that satisfy the provided grammar and constraints, and then pick the most natural repair. We compare LaMirage to state-of-the-art neural and symbolic approaches on 400 real Excel and PowerFx formulas, where LaMirage outperforms all baselines. We release these benchmarks to encourage subsequent work in low-code domains.
summary: Most users of low-code platforms, such as Excel and PowerApps, write programs in domain-specific formula languages to carry out nontrivial tasks. These mistakes, which can be both syntactic and semantic, are hard for low-code users to identify and fix, even though they can be resolved with just a few edits. We formalize the problem of producing such edits as the last-mile repair problem. To address this problem, we developed LaMirage, a LAst-MIle RepAir-engine GEnerator that combines symbolic and neural techniques to perform last-mile repair in low-code formula languages. LaMirage takes a grammar and a set of domain-specific constraints/rules, which jointly approximate the target language, and uses these to generate a repair engine that can fix formulas in that language. To tackle the challenges of localizing the errors and ranking the candidate repairs, LaMirage leverages neural techniques, whereas it relies on symbolic methods to generate candidate repairs. This combination allows LaMirage to find repairs that satisfy the provided grammar and constraints, and then pick the most natural repair. We compare LaMirage to state-of-the-art neural and symbolic approaches on 400 real Excel and PowerFx formulas, where LaMirage outperforms all baselines. We release these benchmarks to encourage subsequent work in low-code domains.
abstract_zh: 大多数低代码平台（例如 Excel 和 PowerApps）的用户都使用特定于领域的公式语言编写程序来执行重要的任务。通常，用户可以编写他们想要的大部分程序，但会引入一些小错误，从而导致公式损坏。这些错误可能是语法上的，也可能是语义上的，对于低代码用户来说很难识别和修复，尽管只需进行一些编辑就可以解决它们。我们将产生此类编辑的问题形式化为最后一英里修复问题。为了解决这个问题，我们开发了 LaMirage，一种最后一英里修复引擎生成器，它结合了符号和神经技术，以低代码公式语言执行最后一英里修复。 LaMirage 采用语法和一组特定于领域的约束/规则，它们共同近似目标语言，并使用它们生成可以修复该语言中的公式的修复引擎。为了解决定位错误和对候选修复进行排名的挑战，LaMirage 利用神经技术，而它依赖于符号方法来生成候选修复。这种组合允许 LaMirage 找到满足所提供的语法和约束的修复，然后选择最自然的修复。我们在 400 个真实的 Excel 和 PowerFx 公式上将 LaMirage 与最先进的神经和符号方法进行比较，其中 LaMirage 优于所有基线。我们发布这些基准测试是为了鼓励低代码领域的后续工作。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种结合神经网络和符号方法的LaMirage系统，用于修复低代码平台中公式语言的语法和语义错误。
AI.motivation: 低代码平台用户常因小错误导致公式失效，而现有方法难以有效定位和修复这些错误，需开发更高效的修复方案。
AI.method: LaMirage通过神经技术定位错误并排序候选修复，利用符号方法生成符合语法规则的修复方案，结合两者优势实现精准修复。
AI.result: 在400个真实Excel和PowerFx公式测试中，LaMirage优于现有神经和符号方法，并发布了相关基准数据集。
AI.conclusion: LaMirage通过神经符号混合方法有效解决低代码公式修复问题，为后续研究提供了新方向和基准数据。

search_text: Neurosymbolic Repair for Low-Code Formula Languages 低代码公式语言的神经符号修复 Most users of low-code platforms, such as Excel and PowerApps, write programs in domain-specific formula languages to carry out nontrivial tasks. Often users can write most of the program they want, but introduce small mistakes that yield broken formulas. These mistakes, which can be both syntactic and semantic, are hard for low-code users to identify and fix, even though they can be resolved with just a few edits. To address this problem, we developed LaMirage, a LAst-MIle RepAir-engine GEnerator that combines symbolic and neural techniques to perform last-mile repair in low-code formula languages. LaMirage takes a grammar and a set of domain-specific constraints/rules, which jointly approximate the target language, and uses these to generate a repair engine that can fix formulas in that language. To tackle the challenges of localizing the errors and ranking the candidate repairs, LaMirage leverages neural techniques, whereas it relies on symbolic methods to generate candidate repairs. This combination allows LaMirage to find repairs that satisfy the provided grammar and constraints, and then pick the most natural repair. We compare LaMirage to state-of-the-art neural and symbolic approaches on 400 real Excel and PowerFx formulas, where LaMirage outperforms all baselines. We release these benchmarks to encourage subsequent work in low-code domains. 大多数低代码平台（例如 Excel 和 PowerApps）的用户都使用特定于领域的公式语言编写程序来执行重要的任务。通常，用户可以编写他们想要的大部分程序，但会引入一些小错误，从而导致公式损坏。这些错误可能是语法上的，也可能是语义上的，对于低代码用户来说很难识别和修复，尽管只需进行一些编辑就可以解决它们。我们将产生此类编辑的问题形式化为最后一英里修复问题。为了解决这个问题，我们开发了 LaMirage，一种最后一英里修复引擎生成器，它结合了符号和神经技术，以低代码公式语言执行最后一英里修复。 LaMirage 采用语法和一组特定于领域的约束/规则，它们共同近似目标语言，并使用它们生成可以修复该语言中的公式的修复引擎。为了解决定位错误和对候选修复进行排名的挑战，LaMirage 利用神经技术，而它依赖于符号方法来生成候选修复。这种组合允许 LaMirage 找到满足所提供的语法和约束的修复，然后选择最自然的修复。我们在 400 个真实的 Excel 和 PowerFx 公式上将 LaMirage 与最先进的神经和符号方法进行比较，其中 LaMirage 优于所有基线。我们发布这些基准测试是为了鼓励低代码领域的后续工作。 本文提出了一种结合神经网络和符号方法的LaMirage系统，用于修复低代码平台中公式语言的语法和语义错误。 Rohan Bavishi,Harshit Joshi,José Pablo Cambronero Sánchez,Anna Fariha,Sumit Gulwani,Vu Le,Ivan Radicek,Ashish Tiwari cs.SE,cs.AI

authors_text: Chang Tian,Wenpeng Yin,Marie-Francine Moens
title: Anti-Overestimation Dialogue Policy Learning for Task-Completion Dialogue System
title_zh: 任务完成对话系统的防高估对话策略学习
abstract: A dialogue policy module is an essential part of task-completion dialogue systems. Recently, increasing interest has focused on reinforcement learning (RL)-based dialogue policy. Its favorable performance and wise action decisions rely on an accurate estimation of action values. The overestimation problem is a widely known issue of RL since its estimate of the maximum action value is larger than the ground truth, which results in an unstable learning process and suboptimal policy. This problem is detrimental to RL-based dialogue policy learning. To mitigate this problem, this paper proposes a dynamic partial average estimator (DPAV) of the ground truth maximum action value. DPAV calculates the partial average between the predicted maximum action value and minimum action value, where the weights are dynamically adaptive and problem-dependent. We incorporate DPAV into a deep Q-network as the dialogue policy and show that our method can achieve better or comparable results compared to top baselines on three dialogue datasets of different domains with a lower computational load. In addition, we also theoretically prove the convergence and derive the upper and lower bounds of the bias compared with those of other methods.
summary: A dialogue policy module is an essential part of task-completion dialogue systems. Recently, increasing interest has focused on reinforcement learning (RL)-based dialogue policy. Its favorable performance and wise action decisions rely on an accurate estimation of action values. The overestimation problem is a widely known issue of RL since its estimate of the maximum action value is larger than the ground truth, which results in an unstable learning process and suboptimal policy. This problem is detrimental to RL-based dialogue policy learning. To mitigate this problem, this paper proposes a dynamic partial average estimator (DPAV) of the ground truth maximum action value. DPAV calculates the partial average between the predicted maximum action value and minimum action value, where the weights are dynamically adaptive and problem-dependent. We incorporate DPAV into a deep Q-network as the dialogue policy and show that our method can achieve better or comparable results compared to top baselines on three dialogue datasets of different domains with a lower computational load. In addition, we also theoretically prove the convergence and derive the upper and lower bounds of the bias compared with those of other methods.
abstract_zh: 对话策略模块是任务完成对话系统的重要组成部分。最近，人们越来越关注基于强化学习（RL）的对话政策。其良好的绩效和明智的行动决策依赖于对行动价值的准确估计。高估问题是强化学习中众所周知的问题，因为它对最大动作值的估计大于真实值，从而导致学习过程不稳定和策略次优。这个问题不利于基于强化学习的对话策略学习。为了缓解这个问题，本文提出了地面真实最大动作值的动态部分平均估计器（DPAV）。 DPAV 计算预测的最大动作值和最小动作值之间的部分平均值，其中权重是动态自适应且取决于问题的。我们将 DPAV 纳入深度 Q 网络作为对话策略，并表明，与不同领域的三个对话数据集上的顶级基线相比，我们的方法可以以较低的计算负载获得更好或可比的结果。此外，我们还从理论上证明了其收敛性，并推导了与其他方法相比偏差的上下界。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种动态部分平均估计器（DPAV），用于解决基于强化学习的对话策略中的过度估计问题，从而提升任务完成型对话系统的性能。
AI.motivation: 强化学习在对话策略学习中因过度估计问题导致学习不稳定和策略次优，亟需有效方法缓解此问题。
AI.method: 设计动态部分平均估计器（DPAV），通过动态调整预测最大动作值与最小动作值的权重，替代传统最大值估计，集成于深度Q网络中。
AI.result: 在三个不同领域的对话数据集上，DPAV方法在保持较低计算负载的同时，表现优于或接近现有最优基线，并理论证明了收敛性及偏差界。
AI.conclusion: DPAV有效缓解了过度估计问题，提升了对话策略的稳定性与性能，且具有理论保障。

authors_text: Lei Zhang,Guanyu Gao,Huaizheng Zhang
title: Spatial-Temporal Federated Learning for Lifelong Person Re-identification on Distributed Edges
title_zh: 分布式边缘上的终身人员重识别的时空联合学​​习
abstract: Data drift is a thorny challenge when deploying person re-identification (ReID) models into real-world devices, where the data distribution is significantly different from that of the training environment and keeps changing. To tackle this issue, we propose a federated spatial-temporal incremental learning approach, named FedSTIL, which leverages both lifelong learning and federated learning to continuously optimize models deployed on many distributed edge clients. Unlike previous efforts, FedSTIL aims to mine spatial-temporal correlations among the knowledge learnt from different edge clients. Specifically, the edge clients first periodically extract general representations of drifted data to optimize their local models. Then, the learnt knowledge from edge clients will be aggregated by centralized parameter server, where the knowledge will be selectively and attentively distilled from spatial- and temporal-dimension with carefully designed mechanisms. Finally, the distilled informative spatial-temporal knowledge will be sent back to correlated edge clients to further improve the recognition accuracy of each edge client with a lifelong learning method. Extensive experiments on a mixture of five real-world datasets demonstrate that our method outperforms others by nearly 4% in Rank-1 accuracy, while reducing communication cost by 62%. All implementation codes are publicly available on https://github.com/MSNLAB/Federated-Lifelong-Person-ReID
summary: Data drift is a thorny challenge when deploying person re-identification (ReID) models into real-world devices, where the data distribution is significantly different from that of the training environment and keeps changing. To tackle this issue, we propose a federated spatial-temporal incremental learning approach, named FedSTIL, which leverages both lifelong learning and federated learning to continuously optimize models deployed on many distributed edge clients. Unlike previous efforts, FedSTIL aims to mine spatial-temporal correlations among the knowledge learnt from different edge clients. Specifically, the edge clients first periodically extract general representations of drifted data to optimize their local models. Then, the learnt knowledge from edge clients will be aggregated by centralized parameter server, where the knowledge will be selectively and attentively distilled from spatial- and temporal-dimension with carefully designed mechanisms. Finally, the distilled informative spatial-temporal knowledge will be sent back to correlated edge clients to further improve the recognition accuracy of each edge client with a lifelong learning method. Extensive experiments on a mixture of five real-world datasets demonstrate that our method outperforms others by nearly 4% in Rank-1 accuracy, while reducing communication cost by 62%. All implementation codes are publicly available on https://github.com/MSNLAB/Federated-Lifelong-Person-ReID
abstract_zh: 将人员重新识别 (ReID) 模型部署到现实设备中时，数据漂移是一个棘手的挑战，其中数据分布与训练环境的数据分布显着不同，并且不断变化。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了一种联邦时空增量学习方法，名为 FedSTIL，它利用终身学习和联邦学习来持续优化部署在许多分布式边缘客户端上的模型。与之前的努力不同，FedSTIL 旨在挖掘从不同边缘客户端学到的知识之间的时空相关性。具体来说，边缘客户端首先定期提取漂移数据的一般表示以优化其本地模型。然后，从边缘客户端学到的知识将由集中式参数服务器聚合，其中知识将通过精心设计的机制从空间和时间维度有选择地、细心地提取。最后，提炼出的信息丰富的时空知识将被发送回相关的边缘客户端，以通过终身学习的方法进一步提高每个边缘客户端的识别精度。对五个真实世界数据集的混合进行的广泛实验表明，我们的方法在 Rank-1 准确率方面比其他方法高出近 4%，同时将通信成本降低了 62%。所有实现代码均可在 https://github.com/MSNLAB/Federated-Lifelong-Person-ReID 上公开获取

AI.tldr: 本文提出FedSTIL方法，通过联邦学习和终身学习结合，解决分布式边缘设备上行人重识别因数据漂移导致的模型性能下降问题。
AI.motivation: 实际部署中行人重识别模型面临数据分布持续变化的挑战，传统方法难以适应动态环境且通信成本高。
AI.method: FedSTIL通过边缘设备周期性提取数据表示优化本地模型，中心服务器采用时空注意力机制蒸馏知识，并将提炼的时空知识反馈至相关设备进行终身学习。
AI.result: 在五个真实数据集上Rank-1准确率提升近4%，通信成本降低62%，代码已开源。
