On a dataset of 49 patients, comprising over 20,000 images, we demonstrate that the use of existing methods as feature extractors results in the effective classification of COVID-19-related pneumonia severity while requiring only minutes of training time. Our methods can achieve an accuracy of over 0.93 on a 4-level severity score scale and provides comparable per-patient region and global scores compared to expert annotated ground truths. These results demonstrate the capability for rapid deployment and use of such minimally-adapted methods for progress monitoring, patient stratification and management in clinical practice for COVID-19 patients, and potentially in other respiratory diseases.
abstract_zh: 基于人工智能的肺部超声成像分析已被证明是在整个 COVID-19 大流行期间快速诊断决策支持的有效技术。然而，此类技术可能需要数天或数周的训练过程和超参数调整来开发智能深度学习图像分析模型。这项工作的重点是利用“现成的”预训练模型作为深度特征提取器，以最少的训练时间对疾病严重程度进行评分。我们建议在简单而紧凑的神经网络之前使用现有方法的预训练初始化，以减少对计算能力的依赖。在时间有限或资源有限的情况下，例如大流行的早期阶段，计算能力的减少至关重要。在包含 49 名患者、包含 20,000 多张图像的数据集上，我们证明，使用现有方法作为特征提取器可以对与 COVID-19 相关的肺炎严重程度进行有效分类，同时只需要几分钟的训练时间。我们的方法可以在 4 级严重程度评分量表上实现超过 0.93 的准确度，并提供与专家注释的基本事实相比可比较的每个患者区域和全局评分。这些结果证明了在 COVID-19 患者的临床实践中以及其他呼吸系统疾病中快速部署和使用这种最低限度适应方法进行进展监测、患者分层和管理的能力。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于预训练模型的资源高效深度特征提取方法，用于快速评估COVID-19患者的肺超声影像严重程度。
AI.motivation: 传统深度学习模型训练耗时且计算资源需求高，难以满足疫情初期资源受限场景下的快速部署需求。
AI.method: 利用预训练模型作为特征提取器，结合轻量级神经网络，在49名患者20,000余张影像数据上进行快速训练与验证。
AI.result: 方法在4级严重程度评分中达到0.93以上准确率，训练时间仅需数分钟，且区域与全局评分与专家标注结果具有可比性。
AI.conclusion: 该方法可有效支持COVID-19患者的病情监测与分层管理，具有临床部署潜力并可扩展至其他呼吸系统疾病。

search_text: Rapid Lung Ultrasound COVID-19 Severity Scoring with Resource-Efficient Deep Feature Extraction 通过资源高效的深度特征提取进行快速肺部超声 COVID-19 严重程度评分 Artificial intelligence-based analysis of lung ultrasound imaging has been demonstrated as an effective technique for rapid diagnostic decision support throughout the COVID-19 pandemic. However, such techniques can require days- or weeks-long training processes and hyper-parameter tuning to develop intelligent deep learning image analysis models. This work focuses on leveraging 'off-the-shelf' pre-trained models as deep feature extractors for scoring disease severity with minimal training time. We propose using pre-trained initializations of existing methods ahead of simple and compact neural networks to reduce reliance on computational capacity. This reduction of computational capacity is of critical importance in time-limited or resource-constrained circumstances, such as the early stages of a pandemic. On a dataset of 49 patients, comprising over 20,000 images, we demonstrate that the use of existing methods as feature extractors results in the effective classification of COVID-19-related pneumonia severity while requiring only minutes of training time. Our methods can achieve an accuracy of over 0.93 on a 4-level severity score scale and provides comparable per-patient region and global scores compared to expert annotated ground truths. These results demonstrate the capability for rapid deployment and use of such minimally-adapted methods for progress monitoring, patient stratification and management in clinical practice for COVID-19 patients, and potentially in other respiratory diseases. 基于人工智能的肺部超声成像分析已被证明是在整个 COVID-19 大流行期间快速诊断决策支持的有效技术。然而，此类技术可能需要数天或数周的训练过程和超参数调整来开发智能深度学习图像分析模型。这项工作的重点是利用“现成的”预训练模型作为深度特征提取器，以最少的训练时间对疾病严重程度进行评分。我们建议在简单而紧凑的神经网络之前使用现有方法的预训练初始化，以减少对计算能力的依赖。在时间有限或资源有限的情况下，例如大流行的早期阶段，计算能力的减少至关重要。在包含 49 名患者、包含 20,000 多张图像的数据集上，我们证明，使用现有方法作为特征提取器可以对与 COVID-19 相关的肺炎严重程度进行有效分类，同时只需要几分钟的训练时间。我们的方法可以在 4 级严重程度评分量表上实现超过 0.93 的准确度，并提供与专家注释的基本事实相比可比较的每个患者区域和全局评分。这些结果证明了在 COVID-19 患者的临床实践中以及其他呼吸系统疾病中快速部署和使用这种最低限度适应方法进行进展监测、患者分层和管理的能力。 本文提出一种基于预训练模型的资源高效深度特征提取方法，用于快速评估COVID-19患者的肺超声影像严重程度。 Pierre Raillard,Lorenzo Cristoni,Andrew Walden,Roberto Lazzari,Thomas Pulimood,Louis Grandjean,Claudia AM Gandini Wheeler-Kingshott,Yipeng Hu,Zachary MC Baum eess.IV,cs.CV

authors_text: Zachary MC Baum,Yipeng Hu,Dean C Barratt
title: Meta-Registration: Learning Test-Time Optimization for Single-Pair Image Registration
title_zh: 元配准：学习单对图像配准的测试时间优化
comment: Accepted to ASMUS 2022 Workshop at MICCAI
comments: Accepted to ASMUS 2022 Workshop at MICCAI
abstract: Neural networks have been proposed for medical image registration by learning, with a substantial amount of training data, the optimal transformations between image pairs. These trained networks can further be optimized on a single pair of test images - known as test-time optimization. This work formulates image registration as a meta-learning algorithm. Such networks can be trained by aligning the training image pairs while simultaneously improving test-time optimization efficacy; tasks which were previously considered two independent training and optimization processes. The proposed meta-registration is hypothesized to maximize the efficiency and effectiveness of the test-time optimization in the "outer" meta-optimization of the networks. For image guidance applications that often are time-critical yet limited in training data, the potentially gained speed and accuracy are compared with classical registration algorithms, registration networks without meta-learning, and single-pair optimization without test-time optimization data. Experiments are presented in this paper using clinical transrectal ultrasound image data from 108 prostate cancer patients. These experiments demonstrate the effectiveness of a meta-registration protocol, which yields significantly improved performance relative to existing learning-based methods. Furthermore, the meta-registration achieves comparable results to classical iterative methods in a fraction of the time, owing to its rapid test-time optimization process.
summary: Neural networks have been proposed for medical image registration by learning, with a substantial amount of training data, the optimal transformations between image pairs. These trained networks can further be optimized on a single pair of test images - known as test-time optimization. This work formulates image registration as a meta-learning algorithm. Such networks can be trained by aligning the training image pairs while simultaneously improving test-time optimization efficacy; tasks which were previously considered two independent training and optimization processes. The proposed meta-registration is hypothesized to maximize the efficiency and effectiveness of the test-time optimization in the "outer" meta-optimization of the networks. For image guidance applications that often are time-critical yet limited in training data, the potentially gained speed and accuracy are compared with classical registration algorithms, registration networks without meta-learning, and single-pair optimization without test-time optimization data. Experiments are presented in this paper using clinical transrectal ultrasound image data from 108 prostate cancer patients. These experiments demonstrate the effectiveness of a meta-registration protocol, which yields significantly improved performance relative to existing learning-based methods. Furthermore, the meta-registration achieves comparable results to classical iterative methods in a fraction of the time, owing to its rapid test-time optimization process.
abstract_zh: 神经网络已被提出用于医学图像配准，通过使用大量训练数据学习图像对之间的最佳变换。这些经过训练的网络可以在一对测试图像上进一步优化 - 称为测试时优化。这项工作将图像配准制定为元学习算法。可以通过对齐训练图像对来训练此类网络，同时提高测试时优化效率；以前被认为是两个独立的训练和优化过程的任务。假设所提出的元注册可以最大化网络“外部”元优化中测试时间优化的效率和有效性。对于通常对时间要求严格但训练数据有限的图像引导应用，将潜在获得的速度和准确性与经典配准算法、没有元学习的配准网络以及没有测试时优化数据的单对优化进行比较。本文使用 108 名前列腺癌患者的临床经直肠超声图像数据进行了实验。这些实验证明了元注册协议的有效性，相对于现有的基于学习的方法，其性能显着提高。此外，由于其快速的测试时间优化过程，元配准在很短的时间内就达到了与经典迭代方法相当的结果。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种称为元配准（Meta-Registration）的方法，通过元学习算法将训练过程与测试时优化结合，提升单对图像配准的效率和效果。
AI.motivation: 传统图像配准方法在训练数据有限或时间敏感的应用（如医学影像）中存在效率与精度不足的问题，需同时优化训练与测试阶段的性能。
AI.method: 通过元学习框架，将图像配准任务建模为元优化问题，使网络在训练时同时学习对齐图像对并提升测试时优化能力，实现训练与测试阶段的联合优化。
AI.result: 在108例前列腺癌患者的临床超声影像数据上，元配准方法相比现有学习方法和经典迭代方法，在速度和精度上均显著提升。
AI.conclusion: 元配准方法有效结合了训练与测试优化，为医学影像等时间敏感且数据有限的应用提供了快速且精确的图像配准解决方案。

ai_content.tldr: 本文提出了一种称为元配准（Meta-Registration）的方法，通过元学习算法将训练过程与测试时优化结合，提升单对图像配准的效率和效果。

authors_text: Zachary MC Baum,Tamas Ungi,Christopher Schlenger,Yipeng Hu,Dean C Barratt
title: Learning Generalized Non-Rigid Multimodal Biomedical Image Registration from Generic Point Set Data
title_zh: 从通用点集数据学习广义非刚性多模态生物医学图像配准
comment: Accepted to ASMUS 2022 Workshop at MICCAI
comments: Accepted to ASMUS 2022 Workshop at MICCAI
abstract: Free Point Transformer (FPT) has been proposed as a data-driven, non-rigid point set registration approach using deep neural networks. As FPT does not assume constraints based on point vicinity or correspondence, it may be trained simply and in a flexible manner by minimizing an unsupervised loss based on the Chamfer Distance. This makes FPT amenable to real-world medical imaging applications where ground-truth deformations may be infeasible to obtain, or in scenarios where only a varying degree of completeness in the point sets to be aligned is available. To test the limit of the correspondence finding ability of FPT and its dependency on training data sets, this work explores the generalizability of the FPT from well-curated non-medical data sets to medical imaging data sets. First, we train FPT on the ModelNet40 dataset to demonstrate its effectiveness and the superior registration performance of FPT over iterative and learning-based point set registration methods. Second, we demonstrate superior performance in rigid and non-rigid registration and robustness to missing data. Last, we highlight the interesting generalizability of the ModelNet-trained FPT by registering reconstructed freehand ultrasound scans of the spine and generic spine models without additional training, whereby the average difference to the ground truth curvatures is 1.3 degrees, across 13 patients.
summary: Free Point Transformer (FPT) has been proposed as a data-driven, non-rigid point set registration approach using deep neural networks. As FPT does not assume constraints based on point vicinity or correspondence, it may be trained simply and in a flexible manner by minimizing an unsupervised loss based on the Chamfer Distance. This makes FPT amenable to real-world medical imaging applications where ground-truth deformations may be infeasible to obtain, or in scenarios where only a varying degree of completeness in the point sets to be aligned is available. To test the limit of the correspondence finding ability of FPT and its dependency on training data sets, this work explores the generalizability of the FPT from well-curated non-medical data sets to medical imaging data sets. First, we train FPT on the ModelNet40 dataset to demonstrate its effectiveness and the superior registration performance of FPT over iterative and learning-based point set registration methods. Second, we demonstrate superior performance in rigid and non-rigid registration and robustness to missing data. Last, we highlight the interesting generalizability of the ModelNet-trained FPT by registering reconstructed freehand ultrasound scans of the spine and generic spine models without additional training, whereby the average difference to the ground truth curvatures is 1.3 degrees, across 13 patients.
abstract_zh: 自由点变换器（FPT）被提出作为一种使用深度神经网络的数据驱动的非刚性点集注册方法。由于 FPT 不假设基于点邻近或对应的约束，因此可以通过最小化基于倒角距离的无监督损失来简单且灵活地训练它。这使得 FPT 适合现实世界的医学成像应用，在这些应用中，地面实况变形可能无法获得，或者在要对齐的点集仅提供不同程度的完整性的情况下。为了测试 FPT 对应查找能力的限制及其对训练数据集的依赖性，本工作探索了 FPT 从精心策划的非医学数据集到医学成像数据集的泛化性。首先，我们在 ModelNet40 数据集上训练 FPT，以证明其有效性以及 FPT 相对于迭代和基于学习的点集配准方法的优越配准性能。其次，我们展示了刚性和非刚性配准的卓越性能以及对缺失数据的鲁棒性。最后，我们强调了 ModelNet 训练的 FPT 的有趣的通用性，通过注册脊柱的重建徒手超声扫描和通用脊柱模型，无需额外的训练，其中 13 名患者与地面真实曲率的平均差异为 1.3 度。

AI.tldr: 本文提出利用Free Point Transformer (FPT)方法，基于通用点集数据学习非刚性的多模态生物医学图像配准，并验证其在医疗数据上的泛化能力。
AI.motivation: 传统医学图像配准方法依赖真实变形数据或点集完整性，而FPT通过无监督训练可适应不完整数据，但其在医疗场景的泛化能力尚未验证。
AI.method: 在ModelNet40数据集训练FPT，对比其与迭代及学习方法的性能；测试刚性/非刚性配准及缺失数据鲁棒性；直接应用训练好的FPT配准脊柱超声图像与模型。
AI.result: FPT在ModelNet40表现优于现有方法，医疗场景下平均曲率误差仅1.3度，且无需额外训练即可处理缺失数据与多模态配准。
AI.conclusion: FPT具备跨领域泛化能力，可有效应用于生物医学图像配准，为无监督医疗影像分析提供新思路。

search_text: Learning Generalized Non-Rigid Multimodal Biomedical Image Registration from Generic Point Set Data 从通用点集数据学习广义非刚性多模态生物医学图像配准 Free Point Transformer (FPT) has been proposed as a data-driven, non-rigid point set registration approach using deep neural networks. As FPT does not assume constraints based on point vicinity or correspondence, it may be trained simply and in a flexible manner by minimizing an unsupervised loss based on the Chamfer Distance. This makes FPT amenable to real-world medical imaging applications where ground-truth deformations may be infeasible to obtain, or in scenarios where only a varying degree of completeness in the point sets to be aligned is available. To test the limit of the correspondence finding ability of FPT and its dependency on training data sets, this work explores the generalizability of the FPT from well-curated non-medical data sets to medical imaging data sets. First, we train FPT on the ModelNet40 dataset to demonstrate its effectiveness and the superior registration performance of FPT over iterative and learning-based point set registration methods. Second, we demonstrate superior performance in rigid and non-rigid registration and robustness to missing data. Last, we highlight the interesting generalizability of the ModelNet-trained FPT by registering reconstructed freehand ultrasound scans of the spine and generic spine models without additional training, whereby the average difference to the ground truth curvatures is 1.3 degrees, across 13 patients. 自由点变换器（FPT）被提出作为一种使用深度神经网络的数据驱动的非刚性点集注册方法。由于 FPT 不假设基于点邻近或对应的约束，因此可以通过最小化基于倒角距离的无监督损失来简单且灵活地训练它。这使得 FPT 适合现实世界的医学成像应用，在这些应用中，地面实况变形可能无法获得，或者在要对齐的点集仅提供不同程度的完整性的情况下。为了测试 FPT 对应查找能力的限制及其对训练数据集的依赖性，本工作探索了 FPT 从精心策划的非医学数据集到医学成像数据集的泛化性。首先，我们在 ModelNet40 数据集上训练 FPT，以证明其有效性以及 FPT 相对于迭代和基于学习的点集配准方法的优越配准性能。其次，我们展示了刚性和非刚性配准的卓越性能以及对缺失数据的鲁棒性。最后，我们强调了 ModelNet 训练的 FPT 的有趣的通用性，通过注册脊柱的重建徒手超声扫描和通用脊柱模型，无需额外的训练，其中 13 名患者与地面真实曲率的平均差异为 1.3 度。 本文提出利用Free Point Transformer (FPT)方法，基于通用点集数据学习非刚性的多模态生物医学图像配准，并验证其在医疗数据上的泛化能力。 Zachary MC Baum,Tamas Ungi,Christopher Schlenger,Yipeng Hu,Dean C Barratt cs.CV,cs.LG

authors_text: Manjeet Kaur,Krishan Kumar Chauhan,Tanya Aggarwal,Pushkar Bharadwaj,Renu Vig,Isibor Kennedy Ihianle,Garima Joshi,Kayode Owa
title: Taguchi based Design of Sequential Convolution Neural Network for Classification of Defective Fasteners
title_zh: 基于田口的缺陷紧固件分类顺序卷积神经网络设计
abstract: Fasteners play a critical role in securing various parts of machinery. Deformations such as dents, cracks, and scratches on the surface of fasteners are caused by material properties and incorrect handling of equipment during production processes. As a result, quality control is required to ensure safe and reliable operations. The existing defect inspection method relies on manual examination, which consumes a significant amount of time, money, and other resources; also, accuracy cannot be guaranteed due to human error. Automatic defect detection systems have proven impactful over the manual inspection technique for defect analysis. However, computational techniques such as convolutional neural networks (CNN) and deep learning-based approaches are evolutionary methods. By carefully selecting the design parameter values, the full potential of CNN can be realised. Using Taguchi-based design of experiments and analysis, an attempt has been made to develop a robust automatic system in this study. The dataset used to train the system has been created manually for M14 size nuts having two labeled classes: Defective and Non-defective. There are a total of 264 images in the dataset. The proposed sequential CNN comes up with a 96.3% validation accuracy, 0.277 validation loss at 0.001 learning rate.
summary: Fasteners play a critical role in securing various parts of machinery. Deformations such as dents, cracks, and scratches on the surface of fasteners are caused by material properties and incorrect handling of equipment during production processes. As a result, quality control is required to ensure safe and reliable operations. The existing defect inspection method relies on manual examination, which consumes a significant amount of time, money, and other resources; also, accuracy cannot be guaranteed due to human error. Automatic defect detection systems have proven impactful over the manual inspection technique for defect analysis. However, computational techniques such as convolutional neural networks (CNN) and deep learning-based approaches are evolutionary methods. By carefully selecting the design parameter values, the full potential of CNN can be realised. Using Taguchi-based design of experiments and analysis, an attempt has been made to develop a robust automatic system in this study. The dataset used to train the system has been created manually for M14 size nuts having two labeled classes: Defective and Non-defective. There are a total of 264 images in the dataset. The proposed sequential CNN comes up with a 96.3% validation accuracy, 0.277 validation loss at 0.001 learning rate.
abstract_zh: 紧固件在固定机械各部件方面发挥着至关重要的作用。紧固件表面的凹痕、裂纹、划痕等变形是由材料特性和生产过程中设备操作不当造成的。因此，需要进行质量控制以确保安全可靠的运行。现有的缺陷检查方法依赖于人工检查，消耗大量的时间、金钱和其他资源；此外，由于人为错误，无法保证准确性。事实证明，自动缺陷检测系统比用于缺陷分析的手动检测技术更有效。然而，卷积神经网络（CNN）和基于深度学习的方法等计算技术是进化方法。通过仔细选择设计参数值，可以充分发挥 CNN 的潜力。本研究尝试使用基于田口的实验和分析设计来开发鲁棒的自动化系统。用于训练系统的数据集是为 M14 尺寸螺母手动创建的，具有两个标记类别：有缺陷和无缺陷。数据集中共有 264 张图像。所提出的顺序 CNN 在 0.001 学习率下的验证准确度为 96.3%，验证损失为 0.277。

AI.tldr: 本文提出基于田口方法的顺序卷积神经网络设计，用于分类有缺陷的紧固件。
AI.motivation: 传统人工检测紧固件缺陷效率低且易出错，需开发自动检测系统以提高准确性和效率。
AI.method: 结合田口实验设计方法优化CNN参数，构建顺序卷积神经网络模型，并使用手动创建的264张M14螺母图像数据集进行训练。
AI.result: 在学习率为0.001时，验证准确率达96.3%，验证损失为0.277。
AI.conclusion: 田口方法优化的CNN模型有效提升了缺陷分类性能，为自动质量检测提供了可靠方案。

ai_content.conclusion: 田口方法优化的CNN模型有效提升了缺陷分类性能，为自动质量检测提供了可靠方案。
